126	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
127	
128	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
129	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
130	open subset of `X`).
131	
132	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
133	
134	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
135	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
136	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
137	
138	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
139	
140	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
141	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
144	§I.3, p. 16). -/
145	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
146	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
147	
148	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
149	of an open). -/
150	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
151	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
152	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
153	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
154	
155	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
156	
157	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
158	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
159	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
160	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
161	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
162	`f.domain`.
163	
164	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
165	conclusion). -/
166	
167	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
168	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
169	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
170	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
171	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
172	
173	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
174	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
175	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
176	
177	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
178	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
179	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
180	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
181	
182	end RationalMap
183	
184	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
185	
186	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
187	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
188	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
189	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
190	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
191	
192	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
193	
194	/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
195	over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
196	*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
197	of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
198	stalk (Stacks `00PD`/`00TT`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
199	`ringKrullDim = 1` has principal nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`),
200	via the cotangent-space `finrank = 1` characterisation.
201	
202	**Iter-179 Lane D body.** The Mathlib bridges
203	- `Algebra.Smooth k A → IsRegularLocalRing A` at every point (Stacks `00TT`
204	  Jacobian criterion side, scheme-level packaging), and
205	- `Order.coheight z = 1 → ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1`
206	  (the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge that
207	  `Scheme.RationalMap.order`'s `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` instance is gated on,
208	  see `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` L148-151)
209	
210	do not ship in Mathlib at commit `b80f227`. We package both as a single
211	internal `sorry` (the `hreg_dim` hypothesis below) and discharge the
212	remaining principal-nonzero conclusion via Mathlib's
213	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` +
214	`IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff` chain. The internal sorry
215	documents exactly the bridge needed: once the smooth-scheme regularity
216	and coheight-Krull-dim instances land in Mathlib (or a project upstream),
217	they discharge `hreg_dim` and the helper closes axiom-clean.
218	
219	The previous body was a bare `sorry`; this Lane-D refactor preserves the
220	warning count at 1 (one sorry per declaration) while exposing the proof
221	skeleton for the next prover. -/
222	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
223	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
224	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
225	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
226	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
227	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
228	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
229	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
230	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
231	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
232	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
233	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
234	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
235	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
236	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
237	  -- The Mathlib-gap content, packaged as a single hypothesis: smoothness +
238	  -- codim-1-witness ⟹ the stalk is a regular local ring of Krull dimension 1.
239	  -- Stacks `00TT` (Jacobian criterion for smoothness ⟹ regular local) and
240	  -- the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge (Stacks `02IZ`/`005X`)
241	  -- together discharge this; neither half ships in Mathlib at b80f227.
242	  have hreg_dim : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ∧
243	                    ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := sorry
244	  obtain ⟨hreg, hdim⟩ := hreg_dim
245	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
246	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
247	  have hfin :
248	      Module.finrank
249	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
250	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
251	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
252	    rw [hdim] at h
253	    exact_mod_cast h
254	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
255	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
256	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
257	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
258	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
259	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
260	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
261	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
262	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
263	    intro hbot
264	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
265	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
266	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
267	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
268	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
269	    rw [hdim] at h0
270	    exact_mod_cast h0
271	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩
272	
273	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
274	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
275	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
276	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
277	
278	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
279	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
280	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
281	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
282	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
283	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
284	
285	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
286	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
287	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
288	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
289	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
290	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
291	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
292	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
293	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
294	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
295	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
296	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
297	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
298	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
299	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
300	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
301	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
302	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
303	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
304	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
305	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
306	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
307	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
308	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
309	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
310	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
311	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
312	    intro hF
313	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
314	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
315	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
316	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)
317	
318	namespace RationalMap

320	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
321	
322	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
323	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
324	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
325	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
326	
327	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
328	
329	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
330	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
331	
332	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
333	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
334	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
335	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
336	
337	The proof combines two steps:
338	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
339	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
340	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
341	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
342	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
343	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
344	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
345	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
346	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
347	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
348	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
349	
350	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
351	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
352	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
353	
354	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
355	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
356	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
357	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
358	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
359	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
360	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
361	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
362	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
363	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
364	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
365	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
366	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
367	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
368	  sorry
369	
370	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
371	
372	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
373	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
374	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
375	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
376	
377	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
378	
379	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
380	codim 1.**
381	
382	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
383	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
384	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
385	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
386	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
387	
388	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
389	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
390	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
391	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
392	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
393	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
394	
395	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
396	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
397	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
398	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
399	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
400	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
401	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
402	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
403	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
404	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
405	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
406	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
407	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
408	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
409	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
410	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) :=
411	  sorry
412	
413	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
414	
415	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
416	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
417	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
418	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
419	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
420	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
421	witnesses are constructed in this project).
422	
423	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
424	
425	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
426	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
427	
428	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
429	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
430	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
431	
432	which requires the pullback machinery
433	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
434	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
435	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
436	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
437	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
438	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
439	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
440	
441	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
442	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
443	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
444	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
445	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
446	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
447	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
448	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
449	the pullback machinery lands.
450	
451	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
452	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
453	
454	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
455	
456	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
457	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
458	are equivalent:
459	
460	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
461	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
462	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
463	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
464	
465	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
466	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
467	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
468	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
469	construct partial-map representatives).
470	
471	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
472	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
473	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
474	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
475	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
476	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
477	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
478	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
479	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
480	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
481	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
482	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
483	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
484	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
485	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
486	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
487	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
488	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
489	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
490	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
491	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
492	
493	end RationalMap
494	
495	end Scheme
496	
497	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
38	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
39	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
40	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
41	statements.
42	
43	The 1 pinned declaration is:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
50	
51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
61	
62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
76	
77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
90	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
91	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
92	-/

106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E helper: the two missing project inputs for `extend_to_av`.**
146	
147	This `sorry`-bodied helper packages the two substantive pieces of mathematical
148	content that the project's current spec does NOT expose as standalone lemmas
149	but that Milne's two-line proof of Theorem 3.2 needs:
150	
151	1. **`IsIntegral A.left`**, the integrality of the abelian variety's
152	   underlying scheme. This is a known Mathlib gap at the project's pinned
153	   commit: smoothness + geometric irreducibility over an algebraically
154	   closed field `k̄` *should* promote to `IsIntegral` (a smooth scheme over
155	   a field is reduced; geometrically irreducible + the single-point base
156	   forces `IrreducibleSpace`; reduced + irreducible ⟹ integral via
157	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`), but the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced`
158	   bridge does not ship in Mathlib at this commit. The project convention
159	   for an abelian variety (`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`'s
160	   module docstring §"Abelian-variety conventions") lists only the four
161	   instances `[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
162	   [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`; `IsIntegral A.left` is "expected from
163	   inferInstance" but in practice requires the missing bridge.
164	
165	2. **`CodimOneFree f`**, i.e. every codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain`.
166	   Milne's proof of Theorem 3.2 derives this by combining
167	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Lemma 3.3 in
168	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` — pure-codim-1-or-empty indeterminacy
169	   for maps into a group variety) with the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne
170	   Theorem 3.1** (the unbundled half of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`,
171	   currently encapsulated INSIDE that theorem's body via the valuative
172	   criterion of properness, but not exposed as a separate lemma). The two
173	   together force the indeterminacy locus empty, hence trivially
174	   `CodimOneFree`.
175	
176	Sketched argument (one-line per piece): by Lemma 3.3 the indeterminacy
177	locus is either empty or of pure codim 1; by Milne 3.1 (the codim-≥2 piece)
178	it has codim ≥ 2; the only set that is both is the empty set, so every
179	codim-1 point lies in `f.domain`.
180	
181	Once Mathlib ships the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` bridge (or the project lifts
182	it as a sibling chapter), and the project exposes the codim-≥2 ruling-out
183	of Milne 3.1 as a separate lemma, this helper closes axiom-clean by
184	inlining the two derivations. Until then, it is the single named `sorry`
185	that breaks the iter-175 → iter-178 four-iter inaction streak on this file
186	while preserving the substantive type signature of `extend_to_av`. -/
187	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
188	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
189	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
190	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
191	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
192	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
193	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
194	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
195	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
196	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
197	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
198	  sorry
199	
200	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
201	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
202	
203	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
204	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
205	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
206	
207	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
208	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
209	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
210	`J → A`.
211	
212	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
213	Milne's two-line argument:
214	
215	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
216	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
217	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
218	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
219	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
220	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
221	   by the caller.
222	
223	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
224	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
225	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
226	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
227	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
228	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
229	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
230	
231	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
232	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
233	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
234	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
235	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
236	
237	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
238	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
239	theorem extend_to_av
240	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
241	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
242	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
243	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
244	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
245	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
246	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
247	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
248	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
249	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
250	  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
251	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
252	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
253	  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
254	  haveI := hint
255	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
256	  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
257	  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod
258	
259	end RationalMap
260	
261	end Scheme
262	
263	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (A.4.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.4.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula and the corollary "regular local ring
14	⟹ Cohen–Macaulay" that the sibling sub-build **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
15	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective surface)
16	consumes.
17	
18	Per STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
19	startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the Albanese
20	geometry and lives entirely under `RingTheory.*`. Mathlib at the project's
21	pinned commit (`b80f227`) exposes `IsRegularLocalRing` and the categorical
22	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on an abelian category (specialised to
23	`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
24	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the
25	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula, and the Cohen–Macaulay predicate are *not* in
26	Mathlib at the pinned commit — they are the new content scaffolded here.
27	
28	## Status (iter-178 update — projectiveDimension closed)
29	
30	This file was originally landed iter-175 (Lane F file-skeleton) with each
31	blueprint-pinned declaration carrying the *intended* substantive type
32	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
33	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex`). The bodies were
34	scheduled for iter-176+ work; the chapter is independent of the rest of
35	Route A's geometric infrastructure, making it a good parallel-work
36	candidate.
37	
38	iter-178 closed `Module.projectiveDimension` kernel-clean as a one-liner
39	re-export of the categorical `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension`. The
40	remaining five typed `sorry` bodies (`depth`, `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`,
41	`depth_of_short_exact`, `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`,
42	`CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) are substantive multi-iter content and stay
43	gated on dedicated body lanes.
44	
45	The 7 pinned declarations are:
46	
47	1. `RingTheory.Module.depth` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the `I`-depth of a
48	   finite `R`-module as the supremum of lengths of `M`-regular sequences in
49	   `I`, valued in `ℕ∞`.
50	2. `RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — for
51	   `(R, 𝔪)` Noetherian local and `M ≠ 0` finite, `depth(M)` equals the
52	   smallest `i` with `Ext^i_R(κ, M) ≠ 0`. Encoded via the depth-bound iff
53	   `Ext` vanishes below.
54	3. `Module.projectiveDimension` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) — an
55	   `R`-module-side wrapper for the categorical
56	   `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)`. The categorical
57	   version exists in Mathlib `b80f227`; this is the re-export pinned by the
58	   blueprint.
59	4. `RingTheory.depth_of_short_exact` (theorem, ~12 LOC) — the
60	   depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequalities (Stacks 00LE).
61	5. `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the formula
62	   `pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)` for a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite
63	   projective dimension over a Noetherian local ring.
64	6. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay` (class, ~3 LOC) — `IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing R`
65	   encoded as `depth(R) = krullDim R`.
66	7. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (theorem, ~6 LOC) — a regular local
67	   ring is Cohen–Macaulay (the consumer-facing input for A.4.a).
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `depth` returns `ℕ∞` and is defined by the regular-sequence supremum.
75	- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is encoded as the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`
76	  vanishing characterisation (the smallest non-vanishing `Ext` index = depth).
77	- `projectiveDimension` re-exports the categorical
78	  `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on `ModuleCat.of R M`.
79	- `depth_of_short_exact` packages the three Stacks 00LE inequalities into a
80	  conjunction.
81	- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` is the numeric equation
82	  `pd + depth = depth(R)`.
83	- `CohenMacaulay` is the equation `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`.
84	- `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is an `IsRegularLocalRing → IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing`
85	  implication, the consumer-facing statement.
86	
87	Unfolding any declaration exposes the named substantive content (a regular-
88	sequence supremum, an `Ext`-vanishing characterisation, the categorical
89	projective dimension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
90	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.
91	
92	## References
93	
94	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` (~560 LOC,
95	7 pins). Stacks Project tags 00LF (definition-depth), 00LP (lemma-depth-ext),
96	00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses), 090V (proposition-Auslander–Buchsbaum), 00N4
97	(definition-local-ring-CM), 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM). Matsumura,
98	*Commutative Ring Theory*, Theorem 19.1. Auslander–Buchsbaum, "Homological

106	open CategoryTheory

112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  sorry
149	
150	end Module
151	
152	end RingTheory

154	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
155	
156	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
157	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
158	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
159	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
160	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
161	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
162	
163	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
164	
165	namespace Module
166	
167	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
168	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
169	
170	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
171	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
172	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
173	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
174	
175	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
176	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
177	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
178	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
179	
180	The body is the one-line re-export
181	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
182	this declaration kernel-clean. -/
183	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
184	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
185	  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)
186	
187	end Module

189	namespace RingTheory

191	namespace Module

193	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
194	
195	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
196	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
197	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
198	
199	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
200	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
201	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
202	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
203	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
204	
205	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
206	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
207	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
208	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
209	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
210	
211	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/
212	
213	/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
214	with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
215	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
216	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
217	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.
218	
219	iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
220	of `Ext^*(κ, -)` applied to `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` for a non-zero-divisor
221	`x ∈ 𝔪`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(κ, M) ≠ 0 ↔ depth(M) = 0`, which is
222	the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
223	`𝔪 ∈ Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC). For the iter-175 file-skeleton
224	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
225	theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
226	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
227	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
228	    [Nontrivial M] (n : ℕ) :
229	    (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ↔
230	      ∀ i : ℕ, i < n →
231	        ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
232	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
233	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 := by
234	  sorry
235	
236	/-! ## §4. Depth on a short exact sequence
237	
238	For a short exact sequence `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` of nonzero finite modules
239	over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
240	crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
241	long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation
242	of §3.
243	
244	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` (Stacks tag 00LE). -/
245	
246	/-- **Depth on a short exact sequence.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
247	ring and let
248	```
249	0 → N' → N → N'' → 0
250	```
251	be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite `R`-modules (encoded by an
252	injection `f : N' →ₗ[R] N`, a surjection `g : N →ₗ[R] N''`, and
253	`Function.Exact f g`). Then
254	1. `depth(N) ≥ min(depth(N'), depth(N''))`,
255	2. `depth(N'') ≥ min(depth(N), depth(N') - 1)`,
256	3. `depth(N') ≥ min(depth(N), depth(N'') + 1)`.
257	
258	The three inequalities are read off from the long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)`
259	sequence applied to the short exact sequence and the depth-via-Ext
260	characterisation (§3 above).
261	
262	iter-176+: the body invokes `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on each module
263	and chases the long exact sequence. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the
264	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
265	theorem depth_of_short_exact
266	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
267	    {N' N N'' : Type u}
268	    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
269	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
270	    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
271	    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
272	    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
273	    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
274	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
275	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
276	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
277	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
278	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
279	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
280	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
281	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
282	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
283	  sorry
284	
285	end Module
286	
287	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
288	
289	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
290	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
291	```
292	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
293	```
294	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
295	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
296	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
297	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
298	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
299	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
300	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
301	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
302	
303	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
304	
305	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
306	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
307	dimension. Then
308	```
309	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
310	```
311	
312	The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
313	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
314	finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ℕ∞`-arithmetic
315	subtleties).
316	
317	iter-176+: the body is the induction on `depth(M)` outlined in the
318	chapter — base case via the minimal-finite-free-resolution +
319	"what is exact" + iterated `Module.depth_of_short_exact`; inductive step
320	via the snake lemma on multiplication by a common non-zero-divisor and
321	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`. For the iter-175
322	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
323	theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
324	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
325	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
326	    [Nontrivial M]
327	    (n : ℕ)
328	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
329	    (n : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
330	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
331	  sorry
332	
333	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
334	
335	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
336	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
337	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
340	
341	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
342	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
343	
344	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
345	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
346	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
347	this is the upstream gap-fill.
348	
349	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
350	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
351	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
352	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
353	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
354	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
355	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
356	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
357	  depth_eq_krullDim :
358	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
359	
360	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
361	
362	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
363	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
364	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
365	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
366	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
367	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
368	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
369	
370	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
371	
372	namespace CohenMacaulay
373	
374	/-- **Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay.** Every regular Noetherian
375	local ring is Cohen–Macaulay: a minimal generating set of `𝔪` is an
376	`R`-regular sequence of length `dim R`, so `depth(R) ≥ dim R`; combined
377	with the standard upper bound `depth(R) ≤ dim R` (Stacks 00LK) this gives
378	`depth(R) = dim R`.
379	
380	This is the consumer-facing input for **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
381	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective
382	surface): the local ring `O_{S,x}` of a regular projective surface at a
383	closed point is regular of Krull dimension `2`, hence Cohen–Macaulay,
384	hence has depth `2`, which is exactly the input the local-cohomology
385	vanishing `H^i_x(O_S) = 0` for `i < 2` needs (Stacks 0AVF; see Hartshorne
386	III.7).
387	
388	iter-176+: the body is either the direct regular-sequence argument
389	outlined in the chapter, or via Auslander–Buchsbaum applied to all finite
390	`R`-modules (any finite `R`-module has `pd_R(M) ≤ d`; specialising to
391	suitable `M` gives the equation). For the iter-175 file-skeleton the body
392	is a typed `sorry`. -/
393	instance of_regular (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
394	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] : CohenMacaulay R where
395	  depth_eq_krullDim := by
396	    sorry
397	
398	end CohenMacaulay
399	
400	end RingTheory

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
64	
65	/-! ### Iter-167 dominance bridge for the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map
66	
67	The four product / Proj instances `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` needs
68	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced` of the product, and
69	`IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) all ship from Lane A
70	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) as the instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
71	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, and `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`. The
72	helper resolves them all by `infer_instance`.
73	
74	The one remaining bridge — dominance of the canonical inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` — is
75	file-local because its proof depends on the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1` (still
76	a Lane A scaffold sorry through iter-167). It is exposed here as a named top-level lemma
77	`iotaGm_isDominant` so the helper carries no inline `sorry`. -/
78	
79	/-- **The canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` is dominant.** Here `ι` is the
80	"specialise the scaling action at `x = 1`" map `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ gmScalingP1`.
81	Once Lane A ships the concrete `gmScalingP1` body (chartwise glue), this becomes the standard
82	open immersion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ ℙ¹` (sending `λ` to `[λ : 1]`), which is dense in the
83	irreducible `ℙ¹`.
84	
85	Project-side bridge pending Lane A's concrete `gmScalingP1` body. -/
86	private lemma iotaGm_isDominant [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
87	    IsDominant (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
88	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left := by

122	/-- **Helper (pointed case): a morphism `f : ℙ¹ → A` killing `0 ∈ ℙ¹` is the constant `1`.**
123	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, if `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` satisfies
124	`ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]`, then `f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)`
125	(equivalently `f = toUnit ProjectiveLineBar ≫ η[A]`).
126	
127	This is the pointed core of the `𝔾ₘ`-scaling shortcut: form `h := gmScalingP1 ≫ f`, feed it
128	to Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) with `V = ProjectiveLineBar`, `W = Gm`, base
129	points `zeroPt`, `onePt`. The `W`-axis collapses by `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, leaving
130	`h = fst ≫ fV` (the relation `f(λx) = fV(x)`). Specialising at `x = 1` (via the canonical
131	inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` given by `λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ`) and using density of `Gm ⊆ ℙ¹` plus
132	separatedness of `A` (via `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`, the same Mathlib bridge
133	`rigidity_core` uses inline), we conclude `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV)`. The basepoint
134	hypothesis then pins `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]`.

136	**Status (iter-167):** body fully refactored — all five in-line product/Proj `sorry`s have
137	been eliminated. Four of them (`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced`
138	of the product, and `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) ship from Lane A
139	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
140	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`) and resolve

142	exposed as the named top-level bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` above (a single `sorry` pending

144	private theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
145	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
146	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
147	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
148	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)
149	    (hf0 : ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]) :
150	    f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
151	  -- W-axis collapse via `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, precomposed with `onePt`:
152	  -- `lift zeroPt onePt ≫ gmScalingP1 = zeroPt`.
153	  have hcollapse :
154	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar) ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar
155	        = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
156	    have hbase := gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero kbar
157	    -- Rewrite `lift zeroPt onePt = onePt ≫ lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)`.
158	    have hreshape :
159	        lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
160	          = Gm.onePt kbar ≫
161	            lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) := by
162	      rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id]
163	      congr 1
164	      rw [← Category.assoc,
165	        toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
166	    rw [hreshape, Category.assoc, hbase, ← Category.assoc,
167	      toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
168	  -- The Cor 1.5 basepoint hypothesis `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ (σ ≫ f) = η[A]`.
169	  have hcorhyp :
170	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
171	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = η[A] := by
172	    rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0
173	  -- Apply Cor 1.5.
174	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
175	    (V := ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (W := Gm kbar)
176	    (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
177	    (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) hcorhyp
178	  -- W-axis restriction: `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ σ ≫ f = (1 : Gm ⟶ A)`.
179	  have hfW :
180	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
181	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = (1 : Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
182	    rw [← Category.assoc, gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero, Category.assoc, hf0]
183	    exact Hom.one_def.symm
184	  -- `snd ≫ fW = snd ≫ 1 = 1` (using `toUnit` uniqueness).
185	  have hSndFW :
186	      snd (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)
187	        ≫ (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
188	          ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f))
189	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
190	    rw [hfW, Hom.one_def, ← Category.assoc]
191	    congr 1
192	    exact toUnit_unique _ _
193	  -- `key` now reads `σ ≫ f = fst ≫ fV` (after collapsing the W-axis to 1).
194	  rw [hSndFW, _root_.mul_one] at key
195	  -- Name the V-axis restriction `fV`.
196	  set fV : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A :=
197	    lift (𝟙 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ Gm.onePt kbar)
198	      ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) with hfVdef
199	  -- Precompose `key` with `gmInP1 := lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) : Gm → ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`
200	  -- ("λ ↦ (1, λ)"). The resulting morphism `ι := gmInP1 ≫ σ : Gm → ℙ¹` is the canonical
201	  -- inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` ("λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ"); on its image, `f` is constant at `onePt ≫ fV`.
202	  set iotaGm : Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
203	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
204	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar with hιdef
205	  have hιf :
206	      iotaGm ≫ f = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
207	    calc iotaGm ≫ f
208	        = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
209	            ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) := by rw [hιdef, Category.assoc]
210	      _ = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
211	            ≫ (fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar) ≫ fV) := by rw [key]
212	      _ = (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
213	            ≫ fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)) ≫ fV := by
214	          rw [Category.assoc]
215	      _ = (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) ≫ fV := by rw [lift_fst]
216	      _ = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
217	          rw [Category.assoc]
218	  -- Separatedness of the target `A` over `Spec k̄` in `OverClass.fromOver` form.
219	  haveI hAsep : IsSeparated A.hom := IsProper.toIsSeparated
220	  haveI : IsSeparated (A.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := hAsep
221	  -- `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and the product geom-irred / LOFT / IsReduced
222	  -- instances are all auto-resolved via Lane A's exports in `Genus0BaseObjects`
223	  -- (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`, `projGm_geomIrred`, `projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`,
224	  -- `projGm_isReduced`). Dominance of `ι.left` is the only file-local bridge, cited
225	  -- explicitly via `iotaGm_isDominant` (the `set`-binding `hιdef` exposes `iotaGm.left`
226	  -- definitionally as `(lift _ _ ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left`).
227	  haveI hιDom : IsDominant iotaGm.left := iotaGm_isDominant
228	  -- Globalise: `ι ≫ f = ι ≫ (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV))` gives, by dominance, `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ c`.
229	  have hf_eq :
230	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
231	    -- Promote the Over morphism equality from the underlying scheme equality.
232	    have hgoal :
233	        iotaGm ≫ f
234	          = iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
235	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)) := by
236	      have hreshape :
237	          iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
238	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV))
239	            = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
240	        rw [← Category.assoc,
241	          toUnit_unique (iotaGm ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (Gm kbar))]
242	      rw [hreshape, hιf]
243	    refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
244	    refine ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
245	      (X := (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left) (Y := A.left)
246	      (f := f.left)
247	      (g := (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
248	        (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)).left) iotaGm.left ?_
249	    rw [← Over.comp_left, ← Over.comp_left]
250	    exact congrArg Over.Hom.left hgoal
251	  -- Pin `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]` via the basepoint hypothesis `hf0`.
252	  have hc : ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV = η[A] := by
253	    have hcomp := hf0
254	    rw [hf_eq] at hcomp
255	    rw [← Category.assoc,
256	      toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
257	        (𝟙 _),
258	      Category.id_comp] at hcomp
259	    exact hcomp
260	  rw [hf_eq, hc, ← Hom.one_def]

262	/-- **A morphism `ℙ¹ → A` is constant.** Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
263	morphism `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` from the projective line into an abelian variety
264	`A` (a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme) is constant: it factors through
265	a single `k̄`-point `a₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ A`, i.e. `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
266	
267	The single-curve base case of Milne's Proposition 3.10. **Route resolved iter-164: the
268	𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut** — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`,
269	char-general. The proof body proceeds by translating in the group `A` to reduce to the
270	basepoint case `zeroPt ≫ f = η[A]` (handled by the helper
271	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`), then un-translating.
272	
273	See blueprint `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`
274	(Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10).
275	
276	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via the helper's residuals
277	(three product-instance Mathlib bridges + dominance of the canonical `Gm → ℙ¹` map). Lifts
278	to axiom-clean once those are discharged. -/
279	theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const
280	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
281	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
282	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
283	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) :
284	    ∃ a₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A,
285	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ a₀ := by
286	  -- Witness: `a₀ := zeroPt ≫ f` (the value of `f` at the scaling fixed point `0 ∈ ℙ¹`).
287	  refine ⟨ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f, ?_⟩
288	  -- Translate: form `f' := f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)` and apply the helper to `f'`.
289	  have hf' :
290	      (f / (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f))
291	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
292	    apply morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
293	    -- Show `zeroPt ≫ (f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)) = η[A]`.
294	    rw [GrpObj.comp_div]
295	    have hv :
296	        ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫
297	            toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
298	              (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f)
299	          = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f := by
300	      rw [← Category.assoc,
301	        toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
302	          (𝟙 _),
303	        Category.id_comp]
304	    rw [hv, div_self', Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
305	  -- Untranslate: `f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀) = 1` ⟺ `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
306	  exact div_eq_one.mp hf'

308	/-- **A genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`.** Over an algebraically closed field
309	`k̄`, a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` with `genus C = 0` is isomorphic — in
310	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))` — to the concrete projective line `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`.
311	
312	Hartshorne's Example IV.1.3.5 (Riemann–Roch). Its formalisation is a genuine sub-build:
313	Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch for curves; this is the dominant long pole flagged by the
314	iter-164 progress-critic.
315	
316	See blueprint `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
317	(Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
318	
319	**Status (iter-166):** signature refactored to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`; body
320	remains `sorry` (RR bridge — iter-167+). -/
321	theorem genusZero_curve_iso_P1
322	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
323	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
324	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
325	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0) :
326	    Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :=
327	  sorry
328	
329	/-- **Headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-`0` curve.** Over an algebraically closed field
330	`k̄` (arbitrary characteristic — no `[CharZero kbar]`), let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically
331	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group
332	scheme). Then every morphism `f : C ⟶ A` killing a `k̄`-point `p` (`p ≫ f = η[A]`) equals the
333	constant morphism at the identity, `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
334	
335	This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that `genusZeroWitness` consumes
336	(via the `k̄ → k` descent step hosted in `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`). Its signature mirrors
337	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` **verbatim except**
338	the `[CharZero kbar]` instance is dropped. Combine `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`C ≅ ℙ¹`) with
339	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (`ℙ¹ → A` constant) and pin the constant value to `η[A]` via
340	the pointed hypothesis. No `df = 0`, no Serre duality, no Picard representability.
341	
342	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
343	(RR bridge, iter-167+) and `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s helper residuals. Once the RR
344	bridge closes and the helper's product-instance + dominance sorries discharge, lifts to
345	axiom-clean. -/
346	theorem rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
347	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
348	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
349	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
350	    [IsProper C.hom]
351	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
352	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
353	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
354	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
355	    (f : C ⟶ A)
356	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
357	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
358	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
359	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
360	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus
361	  set g : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A := φ.inv ≫ f with hgdef
362	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
363	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
364	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
365	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
366	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
367	    exact _hf
368	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
369	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
370	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
371	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
372	    exact hpoint
373	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
374	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
375	  calc f
376	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
377	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
378	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
379	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]
380	    _ = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
381	        rw [← Category.assoc,
382	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]
383	
384	
385	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
