14	/-!
15	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
16	
17	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
18	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
19	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
20	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
21	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
22	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
23	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
24	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
25	
26	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
27	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
28	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
29	
30	This file contains the definition `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` below, the
31	structural-shape acceptance lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`
32	below, and the companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`
33	below (rank = relative dimension `n` from
34	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, closed iter-132).
35	
36	## Status (iter-132 close: rank lemma)
37	
38	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
39	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
40	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
41	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
42	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
43	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
44	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
45	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
46	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
47	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
48	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
49	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
50	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
51	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
52	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
53	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
54	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
55	the outer expression is the explicit
56	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
57	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
58	binder), is preserved. The iter-131 body shape is witnessed by
59	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
60	
61	The **iter-132 prover lane** closed the companion rank lemma
62	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below) against the
63	iter-131 body via a parallel `Classical.choose`-chain extraction on
64	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, reducing through
65	`Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` to pin the
66	`k`-finrank of the cotangent space at the identity to the relative
67	dimension `n` from the `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
68	
69	## References
70	
71	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
72	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
73	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
74	- Project infrastructure consumed:
75	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
76	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
77	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
78	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
79	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via
80	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`):
81	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`,
82	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
83	-/
84	
85	set_option autoImplicit false
86	
87	universe u
88	
89	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
90	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
91	
92	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
93	
94	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
95	
96	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
97	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
98	`ModuleCat k`).
99	
100	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
101	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
102	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
103	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
104	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
105	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
106	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
107	iter-132 companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (at
108	line 244 below) pins the rank to `n` from the
109	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
110	
111	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
112	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
113	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
114	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
115	
116	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
117	change).** The body is built as follows:
118	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
119	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
120	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
121	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
122	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
123	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
124	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
125	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
126	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
127	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
128	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
129	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
130	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
131	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
132	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
133	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
134	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
135	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
136	   `k`-module.
137	
138	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
139	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
140	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
141	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
142	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
143	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
144	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
145	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
146	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
147	the iter-132 rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244
148	below) rewrites against. For the live consumer
149	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
150	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
151	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
152	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
153	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
154	
155	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The rank (`= n`) is
156	now pinned by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below;
157	closed iter-132). The free/finite structural properties
158	(`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`) are content for follow-up companion
159	lemmas (not yet in this file); the structural-shape accessibility needed
160	by those lemmas is witnessed here by
161	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` (line 198 below). -/
162	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
163	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
164	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
165	    ModuleCat k :=
166	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
167	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
168	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
169	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
170	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
171	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
172	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
173	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
174	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
175	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
176	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
177	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
178	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
179	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
180	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
181	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
182	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
183	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
184	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
185	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
186	    exact hxV
187	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
188	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
189	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
190	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
191	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
192	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
193	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
194	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
195	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
196	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
197	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
198	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
199	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
200	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
201	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
202	
203	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
204	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
205	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but
206	the outer `extendScalars`/`ModuleCat.of`-of-`Ω` form is exposed. This proves
207	that the iter-131 body refactor closed the iter-130 opacity defect: a
208	downstream rank lemma can `simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars]`
209	(combined with a `Classical.choose`-chain `obtain` on the same existential)
210	to rewrite the body to the explicit form. -/
211	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
212	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
213	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
214	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
215	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
216	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
217	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
218	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
219	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
220	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
221	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
222	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
223	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
224	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
225	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
226	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
227	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
228	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
229	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
230	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
231	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
232	  exact hxV
233	
234	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
235	
236	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
237	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
238	`finrank` equal to `n`.
239	
240	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
241	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
242	
243	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
244	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
245	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
246	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
247	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
248	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
249	
250	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
251	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
252	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
253	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
254	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
255	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
256	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
257	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
258	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
259	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
260	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
261	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
262	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
263	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
264	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
265	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
266	  let V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose
267	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
268	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
269	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
270	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
271	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
272	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
273	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
274	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
275	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
276	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
277	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
278	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
279	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
280	    exact hxV
281	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
282	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
283	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
284	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
285	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
286	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
287	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
288	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
289	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
290	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
291	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
292	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
293	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
294	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
295	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank

297	/-! ## Piece (i.b) — shear-iso globalisation of the cotangent
298	
299	The next three declarations support the iter-134+ piece (i.b) lane (see
300	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § Piece (i.b)). Their goal is the
301	main lemma `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`:
302	
303	  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom  ≅
304	      pullback_along_str_map (pullback_along_η_G (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))`
305	
306	stated at the **presheaf-of-modules level** (sheaf-level RHS per the iter-133
307	mathlib-analogist Decision 4 + iter-134 plan-agent pre-commitment). The proof
308	chain has three structural pieces:
309	
310	* Step 1 (`shearMulRight`): build the binary-product shear iso
311	  `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in `Over (Spec k)` from `GrpObj` data only.
312	  This is a categorical statement (no scheme-side content); modelled on
313	  `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (one-input version,
314	  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`).
315	
316	* Step 2 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`):
317	  the load-bearing presheaf-of-modules base-change identity
318	  `Ω_{(G ⊗ G)/G} ≅ pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` on `(G ⊗ G).left`.
319	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (~150–300 LOC); chains
320	  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv` (algebra side) with
321	  `PresheafOfModules.pullback` / `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` (presheaf side).
322	
323	* Step 3 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`):
324	  the section-restriction identity bridging `s^* pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` to
325	  `π_G^* η_G^* Ω_{G/k}` via `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` and the
326	  categorical identity `pr_2 ∘ s = η_G ∘ π_G` for `s = ⟨𝟙_G, η_G⟩`.
327	-/
328	
329	section ShearIso
330	
331	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
332	
333	/-- **Binary-product shear isomorphism** for a group object `G` in a Cartesian
334	monoidal category: the map
335	`σ = lift (fst G G) μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G`, informally `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b)`,
336	is an isomorphism. The inverse is
337	`σ⁻¹ = lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`, informally
338	`(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b)`.
339	
340	This is the binary-product upgrade of
341	`CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`),
342	which packages only the one-input version `(· · f)` for a fixed global element
343	`f : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`. Here both factors of `G ⊗ G` are free.
344	
345	Piece (i.b) Step 1 (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` Step 1 of
346	`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`). NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (no packaged shear
347	iso for binary products in Mathlib; the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict
348	Decision 1 prescribes this exact construction). -/
349	@[simps]
350	def shearMulRight (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G where
351	  hom := lift (fst G G) μ
352	  inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)
353	  hom_inv_id := by
354	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · (a · b)) = (a, b)`.
355	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
356	    · simp
357	    · -- Goal: `(σ.hom ≫ σ.inv) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
358	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
359	      -- Goal: `σ.hom ≫ (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) = snd G G`
360	      rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]
361	      -- Goal: `lift (σ.hom ≫ (fst G G ≫ ι)) (σ.hom ≫ snd G G) ≫ μ = snd G G`
362	      rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
363	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ = snd G G`
364	      -- Trick: use `lift_lift_assoc` with f := fst ≫ ι, g := fst, h := snd, then
365	      -- close by `lift_comp_inv_left` and `lift_comp_one_left`.
366	      rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
367	          lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by
368	            rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
369	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left, MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
370	  inv_hom_id := by
371	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b) ↦ (a, a · (a⁻¹ · b)) = (a, b)`.
372	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
373	    · simp
374	    · -- Goal: `(σ.inv ≫ σ.hom) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
375	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
376	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ = snd G G`
377	      -- Apply `lift_lift_assoc` (← direction): the RHS form `lift f (lift g h ≫ μ) ≫ μ`
378	      -- rewrites to `lift (lift f g ≫ μ) h ≫ μ` with f := fst, g := fst ≫ ι, h := snd.
379	      rw [show (lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ :
380	          G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
381	          lift (lift (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from
382	          (MonObj.lift_lift_assoc (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G)).symm,
383	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_right,
384	        MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
385	
386	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
387	lemma shearMulRight_hom_fst (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
388	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ fst G G = fst G G := by
389	  simp [shearMulRight]
390	
391	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
392	lemma shearMulRight_hom_snd (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
393	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ snd G G = μ := by
394	  simp [shearMulRight]
395	
396	end ShearIso
397	
398	/-! ### Helpers / main lemma for piece (i.b)
399	
400	The remaining declarations of this section state the helper sub-lemmas
401	and main lemma of piece (i.b) at the presheaf-of-modules level. Their
402	bodies are work-in-progress for iter-134+; the proofs decompose
403	along the closure path documented in
404	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § Piece (i.b) +
405	`analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`.
406	-/
407	
408	/-- **Compatibility morphism of structure presheaves of rings** along a scheme
409	morphism `f`. This is the `φ' = ((adj).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c` shape used by
410	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`; we package it once here so the
411	`PresheafOfModules.pullback`-based statements below can refer to it
412	uniformly.
413	
414	Specifically, for a scheme morphism `f : Y ⟶ Z`, this is the adjunction
415	transpose of the structure-sheaf morphism `f.c : O_Z ⟶ f.base _* O_Y`,
416	delivering a morphism `f.base⁻¹ O_Z ⟶ O_Y` of presheaves of rings on
417	`Y`.
418	
419	**Note**: this is **not** the φ for `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, which
420	expects a morphism of presheaves on the *codomain* `Y`
421	(`Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ (Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf`) and is
422	obtained via `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom`. The two conventions
423	serve distinct downstream consumers. -/
424	noncomputable def schemeHomRingCompatibility {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ Z) :
425	    (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback (C := CommRingCat) f.base).obj Z.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
426	  ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c
427

453	/-- The categorical identity at the underlying scheme level:

461	      G.hom ≫ (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) := by

463	  rfl

491	
492	1. `d_app` (algebraic-coherence): for `a : ((G.hom.base⁻¹ O_{Spec k})).obj X`,

515	"PRIMARY: build `pullbackObjEquivTensor` helper" suggested order *vs.* the
516	"FALLBACK: Route (b) Inverse-direction-via-adjunction-transpose" — the

524	Decision 2 + `analogies/kaehler-tensorequiv-presheafpullback.md` 5-step

526	
527	/-- **Iso-reflection bridge for `PresheafOfModules` morphisms** (iter-140 Route
528	(b'2) closure helper, per `analogies/isiso-basechange-along-proj-two-inv.md`
529	Decision 2). A morphism `f : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of modules over a presheaf
530	of rings `R` is an isomorphism iff its component `f.app X` is an iso in
531	`ModuleCat (R.obj X)` for every open `X`.
532	
533	Chains two Mathlib facts: `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R` reflects isomorphisms
534	(via `Balanced` + `Faithful` ⇒ `ReflectsMonomorphisms` + `ReflectsEpimorphisms`
535	⇒ `ReflectsIsomorphisms`, from
536	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced`), and
537	`NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app` localises iso-checks on natural transformations
538	to pointwise iso-checks.
539	
540	Mirrors `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
541	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:132`) for the
542	`PresheafOfModules` (rather than `SheafOfModules`) category.
543	Upstream-PR candidate. -/
544	private theorem isIso_of_app_iso_module {C : Type*} [Category C]
545	    {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {M N : PresheafOfModules R}
546	    (f : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ X, IsIso (f.app X)) : IsIso f := by
547	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R),
548	      NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
549	  intro X
550	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat) (f.app X)
551	
552	-- iter-145 EXCISE: `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`,
553	-- `basechange_along_proj_two_inv`, `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso`,
554	-- and `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two` were
555	-- excised in iter-145 per the chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-excise
556	-- refactor directive (the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot descoped the
557	-- bundled-route route (b'2) IsIso chain; iter-145 strategy-critic Q7
558	-- ruled the "auditable record" disposition was the iter-141 sunk-cost
559	-- pattern recurring). See `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`
560	-- for the iter-145+ chart-algebra route 5-piece skeleton.
561	/-- **Restriction of the relative differentials presheaf along the canonical
562	section** `s := ⟨𝟙_G, η_G⟩ : G ⟶ G ⊗ G` of the first projection in
563	`Over (Spec k)` (piece (i.b) Step 3).
564	
565	Signature is the intended sheaf-level section-restriction iso, per
566	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` Step 3 of
567	`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` (`lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section`):
568	
569	  `s^*(pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}) ≅ π_G^*(η_G^* Ω_{G/k})`
570	
571	obtained from the categorical identity `pr_2 ∘ s = η_G ∘ π_G` (where
572	`π_G : G ⟶ Spec k` is the structure map and `pr_2 : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` the second
573	projection). The compatibility morphisms for the four scheme morphisms
574	involved are spelled inline via `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom _).hom`.
575	
576	**Iter-136 closure (Step 3)**: closed via the categorical identity
577	`s.left ≫ pr_2.left = G.hom ≫ η[G].left` (`section_snd_eq_identity_struct`
578	above) combined with `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` on both sides. -/
579	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section
580	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G]
581	    {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
582	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
583	    (PresheafOfModules.pullback
584	          (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
585	            (lift (𝟙 G) (toUnit G ≫ η[G])).left).hom).obj
586	        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback
587	            (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (snd G G).left).hom).obj
588	          (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) ≅
589	      (PresheafOfModules.pullback
590	          (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom G.hom).hom).obj
591	        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback
592	            (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
593	              (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G])).hom).obj
594	          (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) := by
595	  -- Step 1. Use `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` to merge each side's nested pullback
596	  -- into a single pullback along the composed compatibility morphism.
597	  -- Step 2. The composed compatibility morphisms on each side are *definitionally*
598	  -- equal to `(toRingCatSheafHom (lift.left ≫ snd.left)).hom` and
599	  -- `(toRingCatSheafHom (G.hom ≫ η[G].left)).hom` respectively (by Scheme c-composition
600	  -- `LocallyRingedSpace.comp_c` + commutation of `whiskerLeft`/`whiskerRight`).
601	  -- Step 3. `section_snd_eq_identity_struct` identifies the two single-morphism pullbacks.
602	  have iso1 := (PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp
603	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (snd G G).left).hom
604	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (lift (𝟙 G) (toUnit G ≫ η[G])).left).hom).app
605	    (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)
606	  have iso2 := (PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp
607	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G])).hom
608	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom G.hom).hom).app
609	    (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)
610	  refine iso1 ≪≫ ?_ ≪≫ iso2.symm
611	  refine eqToIso ?_
612	  -- After `pullbackComp` both sides reduce to single-morphism pullbacks; the c-composition
613	  -- rule lets us re-express them as `pullback (toRingCatSheafHom <composite>).hom`.
614	  change (PresheafOfModules.pullback
615	        (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
616	          ((lift (𝟙 G) (toUnit G ≫ η[G])).left ≫ (snd G G).left)).hom).obj
617	      (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom) =
618	    (PresheafOfModules.pullback
619	        (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
620	          (G.hom ≫ (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]))).hom).obj
621	      (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)
622	  rw [section_snd_eq_identity_struct]
623	
624	-- iter-145 EXCISE: `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` (the iter-135 piece (i.b)
625	-- Main scaffold) was excised per the iter-145 chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-
626	-- excise refactor directive. Its sorry-body was load-bearing only for the
627	-- bundled-route closure path, which iter-144 descoped via the chart-algebra
628	-- pivot. See `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` for the
629	-- iter-145+ chart-algebra route 5-piece skeleton.
630	
631	end AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj

15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	/-- The Kähler module of a localization is subsingleton.
69	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. -/
70	theorem kaehler_localization_subsingleton
71	    {A L : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [Algebra A L]
72	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
73	    Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) :=
74	  letI : Algebra.FormallyUnramified A L :=
75	    Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization (R := A) (Rₘ := L) M
76	  Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential
77	
78	/-- Tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv` for the Kähler module under a localization:
79	if `A → L` is a localization at `M ⊆ A` and `L → B` is a ring map forming a
80	scalar tower `A → L → B`, then the canonical map `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/L]` is a
81	`B`-linear equivalence.
82	
83	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` and the most
84	extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1 (candidate name
85	`KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`). -/
86	noncomputable def kaehler_quotient_localization_iso
87	    {A L B : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [CommRing B]
88	    [Algebra A L] [Algebra A B] [Algebra L B] [IsScalarTower A L B]
89	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
90	    Ω[B⁄A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄L] := by
91	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) := kaehler_localization_subsingleton M
92	  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective (KaehlerDifferential.map A L B B) ⟨?_, ?_⟩
93	  · rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
94	    intro x hx
95	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ :=
96	      (KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map A L B x).mp hx
97	    -- y : B ⊗[L] Ω[L⁄A]; since Ω[L⁄A] is subsingleton, the tensor product is too
98	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A])) := by
99	      refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
100	      have hzero : ∀ z : TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A]), z = 0 := by
101	        intro z
102	        induction z with
103	        | zero => rfl
104	        | tmul x y =>
105	          rw [show y = (0 : Ω[L⁄A]) from Subsingleton.elim _ _, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
106	        | add x y hx hy => rw [hx, hy, add_zero]
107	      rw [hzero a, hzero b]
108	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
109	  · exact KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective _ _ _
110	
111	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
112	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
113	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
114	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
115	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
116	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
117	
118	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
119	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
120	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
121	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
122	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
123	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
124	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
125	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
126	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
127	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
128	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
129	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
130	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
131	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
132	  intro x
133	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
134	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
135	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
136	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
137	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
138	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
139	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
140	      first
141	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
142	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
143	
144	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
145

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry
46

8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.Points
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.GmScaling

11	/-!
12	# Genus-`0` base objects — re-export shim
13	
14	Iter-175 split this file into four sub-modules. All declarations are re-exported
15	from their new homes via `import` (Lean has no `export` ceremony for non-namespaced
16	decls — re-export is automatic from imports).
17	
18	* `BareScheme.lean` — `ProjectiveLineBar` scheme + the 2-chart affine cover.
19	* `ChartIso.lean`   — `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` chart-ring iso
20	                     + its load-bearing helpers.

22	* `GmScaling.lean`  — chart-bridge + `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` action + product-stability

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
13	
14	* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	set_option linter.style.setOption false
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
32	
33	noncomputable section
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
152	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
153	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
154	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
155	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
156	  sorry
157	
158	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
159	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
160	acceptable for iter-165). -/
161	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
162	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
163	  sorry
164	
165	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
166	
167	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
168	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
169	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
170	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
171	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
172	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
173	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
174	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
175	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
176	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
177	  fun i => by
178	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
179	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
180	
181	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
182	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
183	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
184	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
185	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
186	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
187	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
188	
189	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
190	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
191	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
192	
193	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
194	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
195	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
196	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
197	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
198	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
199	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
200	    (m := ![1, 1])
201	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
202	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
203	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
204	    (by
205	      classical
206	      intro p hp
207	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
208	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
209	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
210	        have := hp
211	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
212	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
213	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
214	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
215	        exact this
216	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
217	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
218	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
219	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
220	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
221	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
222	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
223	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
224	        by_contra h
225	        push Not at h
226	        apply hd_ne
227	        ext k
228	        fin_cases k
229	        · simpa using h.1
230	        · simpa using h.2
231	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
232	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
233	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
234	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
235	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
236	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
237	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
238	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
239	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
240	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
241	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
242	
243	end AlgebraicGeometry
244	
245	end

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 2): the chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 2** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships the
13	load-bearing chart-ring iso
14	
15	```
16	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i)
17	  ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar
18	```
19	
20	together with the `kbar`-algebra preservation lemma. The forward direction is via
21	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`; the inverse via
22	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i = isLocalizationElem`.
23	
24	Upstream stratum: `BareScheme`. Downstream strata: `Points`, `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
39	
40	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
41	def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
42	  | 0 => 1
43	  | 1 => 0
44	
45	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
46	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
47	
48	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
49	  fin_cases i <;> decide
50	
51	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
52	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
53	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
54	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
55	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
56	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
57	
58	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
59	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
60	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
61	
62	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
63	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
64	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
65	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
66	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
67	
68	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
69	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
70	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
71	
72	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
73	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
74	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
75	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
76	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
77	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
78	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
79	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
80	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
81	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
82	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
83	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
84	
85	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
86	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
87	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
88	    kbar →+*
89	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
90	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
91	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
92	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
93	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
94	
95	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
96	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
97	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
98	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
99	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
100	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
101	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
102	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
103	    (fun _ : Unit =>
104	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
105	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
106	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
107	
108	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
109	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
110	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
111	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
112	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
113	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
114	  · intro r
115	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
116	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
117	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
118	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
119	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
120	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
121	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
122	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
123	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
124	      MvPolynomial.C r
125	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
126	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
127	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
128	  · intro _
129	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
130	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
131	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
132	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
133	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
134	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
135	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
136	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
137	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
138	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
139	      MvPolynomial.X ()
140	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
141	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
142	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
143	
144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
151	
152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
157	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
159	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
167	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
168	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
169	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
170	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
171	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
176	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
177	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
178	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
179	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
180	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
181	    apply top_unique
182	    intro p _
183	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
184	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
185	      intro r
186	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
187	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
188	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
189	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
190	      rw [← h]
191	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
192	    · -- add case
193	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
194	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
195	    · -- mul_X case
196	      intro p₁ j hp₁
197	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
198	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
199	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
200	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
201	    v hx dv hxd
202	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
203	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
204	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
205	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
206	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
207	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
208	    apply Subtype.ext
209	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
210	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
211	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
212	    exact key
213	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
214	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
215	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
216	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
217	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
218	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
219	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
220	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
221	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
222	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
223	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
224	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
225	      have h := hai
226	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
227	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
228	      omega
229	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
230	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
231	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
232	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
233	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
234	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
235	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
236	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
237	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
238	    simp only [pow_one]
239	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
240	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
241	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
242	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
243	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
244	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
245	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
246	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
247	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
248	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
249	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
250	      subst heq_i
251	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
252	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
253	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
254	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
255	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
256	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
257	      subst heq_i
258	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
259	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
260	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
261	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
262	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
263	  intro y
264	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
265	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
266	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
267	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
268	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
269	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
270	    rw [htop]; trivial
271	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
272	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
273	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
274	  · -- mem case
275	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
276	    have hgen :=
277	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
278	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
279	    rw [hgen]
280	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
281	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
282	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
283	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
284	    rw [map_pow, hX]
285	  · -- algebraMap case
286	    intro r
287	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
288	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
289	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
290	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
291	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
292	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
293	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
294	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
295	    rw [hr₀]
296	    rfl
297	  · -- add case
298	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
299	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
300	  · -- mul case
301	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
302	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
303	
304	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
305	
306	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
307	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
308	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
309	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
310	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
311	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
312	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
313	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
314	      RingHom.id _ := by
315	  ext x
316	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
317	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
318	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
319	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
320	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
321	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
322	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
323	
324	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl
377	
378	end AlgebraicGeometry
379	
380	end
381
