6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
241	
242	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
243	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
244	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
245	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection). Scaffold body — same
246	discipline as `ga_grpObj`.
247	
248	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
249	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
250	`W = Gm`). -/
251	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
252	
253	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
254	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
255	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
256	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
257	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
258	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
259	
260	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
261	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
262	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
263	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
264	  η[Gm kbar]
265	
266	end AlgebraicGeometry
267	
268	end
269

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
64	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
65	  [IsProper C.hom]
66	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
67	
68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
78	
79	namespace IsAlbanese
80	
81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
91	
92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
99	
100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
129	
130	end IsAlbanese
131	
132	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
133	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
134	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
135	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
136	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
137	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
138	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
139	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
140	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
141	
142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
175	
176	/-- Genus-`0` Albanese witness. The witness object is the trivial terminal scheme
177	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec k))`; the `JacobianWitness` structural fields (proper, smooth,
178	geometrically irreducible, genus-stable, group-object) reduce to the
179	corresponding trivialities on `Spec k` via the structural cluster
180	`(𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)`. The `isAlbaneseFor` field consumes the **genus-0
181	abelian-variety rigidity** statement: for every pointed `(C, P)` and every
182	target group scheme `(A, η[A])`, the genus-0 hypothesis forces a morphism
183	`f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]` to equal `toUnit C ≫ η[A]`. Over an
184	algebraically closed base `k̄` this is
185	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (in
186	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, char-free, route-C, iter-163).
187	For a general `[Field k]` base the keystone is consumed via pullback to
188	`Spec k̄` + descent of the morphism equality along the faithfully-flat
189	surjection `Spec k̄ → Spec k`. The uniqueness clause cancels the epimorphism
190	`toUnit C` via `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective` (smooth ⟹ flat,
191	geometrically irreducible ⟹ surjective on `Spec k`).
192	
193	**Status (iter-172):** the `key` body remains `sorry`; the rigidity consumer
194	exists (declared) but still propagates upstream sorries from the iter-171
195	body skeleton in `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`. Wiring closes once
196	(i) the `Genus0BaseObjects` body-skeleton internal sorries discharge, and
197	(ii) the `k → k̄` pullback / descent step is filled. -/
198	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
200	    (h : genus C = 0) :
201	    JacobianWitness C where
202	  J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
203	  grpObj := inferInstance
204	  -- `(𝟙_).hom` is defeq `𝟙 (Spec k)`, but instance search does not unfold it;
205	  -- supply each instance for the identity and let term-elaboration close by defeq.
206	  proper := (inferInstance : IsProper (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
207	  smooth := (inferInstance : Smooth (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
208	  geomIrred := geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k
209	  smoothGenus := by
210	    rw [h]; exact (inferInstance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
211	  isAlbaneseFor := by
212	    -- For every `k`-rational marked point `P`, exhibit the Albanese datum for
213	    -- the pointed curve `(C, P)` with `J = 𝟙_` (blueprint C.2 / C.3).
214	    intro P
215	    -- The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map `toUnit C`.
216	    refine ⟨toUnit C, ?_, ?_⟩
217	    · -- Pointed condition: both `P ≫ toUnit C` and `η[𝟙_]` are maps `𝟙_ ⟶ 𝟙_`,
218	      -- equal by uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object.
219	      exact toUnit_unique _ _
220	    · -- Universal factorisation: given `f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]`, the
221	      -- factoring morphism `𝟙_ ⟶ A` is the identity element `η[A]`.
222	      intro A _ _ _ _ f _hf
223	      -- Genus-0 RIGIDITY (C.2): `f` equals the constant morphism at the identity.
224	      -- The route-C content `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (declared in
225	      -- `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`) is char-free and import-clean
226	      -- (proven iter-163 onward). To consume it for the general `[Field k]` goal,
227	      -- this body needs to pull back `(C, A, f, P)` along an algebraic closure
228	      -- `Spec k̄ → Spec k`, apply `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` in the
229	      -- pulled-back setting, then descend the morphism equality along the
230	      -- faithfully-flat surjection (the descent step itself is
231	      -- `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`, already used below to cancel the
232	      -- terminal epimorphism). The pullback functor + the descent of a morphism
233	      -- equality is the residual sub-build; it is real but is not gated on
234	      -- any out-of-file plan-level decision.
235	      have key : f = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
236	        sorry
237	      refine ⟨η[A], key, ?_⟩
238	      -- Uniqueness: any `g'` with `f = toUnit C ≫ g'` equals `η[A]`, by cancelling
239	      -- the epimorphism `toUnit C`. Its underlying scheme morphism is `C.hom`
240	      -- (`Over.toUnit_left`), which is faithfully flat (smooth ⟹ flat) and
241	      -- surjective (a geometrically irreducible curve over a field is nonempty and
242	      -- `Spec k` is a single point), hence an epimorphism (`Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`).
243	      intro g' hg'
244	      have hcancel : toUnit C ≫ g' = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by rw [← hg', ← key]
245	      have hepiL : Epi (toUnit C).left := by
246	        rw [Over.toUnit_left]; exact Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective C.hom
247	      haveI : Epi (toUnit C) := Over.epi_of_epi_left _
248	      exact (cancel_epi (toUnit C)).mp hcancel
249	
250	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
251	of positive genus `g ≥ 1` over `k`. The underlying scheme is the Albanese
252	variety of `C` — classically the connected component of the identity of
253	`Pic_{C/k}`, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel–Jacobi
254	morphism on `Sym^g(C)`. Both constructions require infrastructure not yet
255	in Mathlib (FGA representability of the Picard functor for Route A;
256	symmetric powers + finite-group quotients + Stein factorisation +
257	Brill–Noether–Riemann–Roch for Route B); the iter-123 M3 route audit
258	(`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`) records both at midpoint ~6500 and
259	~9000 LOC respectively.
260	
261	**Status**: iter-134 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is M3
262	work, currently OFF-CRITICAL-PATH until M2 closes (per STRATEGY.md
263	§ M3, user-escalation-pending).
264	
265	**Iter-135**: the genus-stratified body restructure of
266	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is now in place (Change 2.1 of iter-135
267	refactor); this scaffold is the positive-genus arm. Body closure
268	remains M3 work (off-critical-path; user-escalation-pending per
269	`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`). -/
270	noncomputable def positiveGenusWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
271	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
272	    (hg : 0 < genus C) :
273	    JacobianWitness C :=
274	  sorry
275	
276	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
277	curve. This is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C Jacobian
278	scaffolding (the other being `genusZeroWitness`); both are scheduled for body
279	closure post M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. It packages all five protected sorries
280	(`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
281	`instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records
282	that the Albanese property of the underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every
283	choice of $k$-rational marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor`
284	field of `JacobianWitness`).
285	
286	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
287	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
288	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
289	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
290	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
291	existence is assumed.
292	
293	**Iter-135 body restructure**: the body is now a `by_cases h :
294	genus C = 0` decomposition consuming `genusZeroWitness` (genus-0 arm)
295	and `positiveGenusWitness` (positive-genus arm). Both arms' bodies
296	remain `sorry` (M2.b body closure iter-138+; M3 body closure
297	off-critical-path); the restructure converts the inline `sorry` here
298	into honest delegation to the two scaffolds, removing one inline-`sorry`
299	site without prejudging either arm's body closure. -/
300	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
301	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
302	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := by
303	  by_cases h : genus C = 0
304	  · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩
305	  · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩
306	
307	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
308	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
309	on it. -/
310	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
311	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
312	    JacobianWitness C :=
313	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
314	
315	-- data
316	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
317	
318	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
319	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
320	witness is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C scaffolding
321	(the other being `genusZeroWitness`), both scheduled for body closure post
322	M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the
323	witness — a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of
324	relative dimension `0` is `Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is
325	absorbed into `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
326	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
327	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
328	  (jacobianWitness C).J
329	
330	namespace Jacobian
331	
332	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
333	
334	-- data
335	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
336	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
337	
338	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
339	genus of `C`. -/
340	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
341	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
342	
343	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
344	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
345	
346	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
347	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
348	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
349	
350	end Jacobian
351	
352	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
12	project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
13	Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
14	étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
15	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
16	`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
17	Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).
18	
19	## Status (iter-176 Lane I file-skeleton)
20	
21	This file is the **iter-176 Lane I** file-skeleton (re-dispatch of the iter-175
22	Lane I that died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without ever
23	calling `Write`). Each of the five blueprint-pinned declarations carries the
24	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
25	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`) with a `sorry`
26	body. The bodies are iter-177+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
27	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` (A.2.b)
28	landing their pinned declarations.
29	
30	The 5 pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
33	   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
34	   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
35	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
36	   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
37	   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
38	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
39	   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
40	   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
41	   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
42	   representing quotient `Q`.
43	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
44	   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
45	   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
46	   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
47	   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
48	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
49	   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
50	   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
51	   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
52	
53	## Note on type expressivity
54	
55	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
56	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
57	
58	- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
59	  tautological `Spec k`.
60	- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
61	  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
62	  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
63	- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
64	  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
65	  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
66	  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
67	  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
68	- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
69	  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
70	  `picSharp C |_T`.
71	- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
72	  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
73	  group axioms.
74	
75	The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders
76	for the sibling-chapter functors `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` and `Div_{C/k}`) are
77	typed-`sorry` *carriers* used to express the 5 pinned declarations' types
78	without forward-referencing into Lean files that have not yet landed. They
79	ship in the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` namespace, marked with
80	docstrings explaining they will be re-exports of the sibling-chapter
81	landing once `Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` land
82	(forward refs `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp` and
83	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor` per their blueprint `\lean{...}`
84	pins). Their bodies collapse to a one-line `:= ...` re-export at that
85	point.
86	
87	## References

154	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
155	
156	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
157	`C/k` is the `k`-scheme representing the étale-sheafified relative Picard
158	functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`. It is supplied by Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`
159	(specialised to `S = Spec k`, `X = C` via Cor. `cor:algsch`); the
160	representability theorem itself is the next declaration (`representable`),
161	and `PicScheme` is the witness scheme extracted from it.
162	
163	The scheme is separated, locally of finite type over `k`, and a disjoint
164	union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the abelian-group-scheme
165	structure is the content of `groupSchemeStructure`.
166	
167	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
168	
169	/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically
170	integral curve `C/k`.
171	
172	Encoded as an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))` (a `k`-scheme), carrying the
173	substantive identity "this is the scheme representing `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`"
174	via the `RepresentableBy` witness produced by
175	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable`. The structural
176	properties (separated, locally of finite type, disjoint union of open
177	quasi-projectives) live on the underlying scheme via the standard
178	instances of `Over (Spec (.of k))`.
179	
180	iter-177+: the body extracts the representing object from

182	`(representable C).choose` modulo a `Functor.RepresentableBy` witness; or

186	body is a typed `sorry`. -/

188	    (_C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
189	  sorry

193	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
194	
195	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
196	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` (an `S`-flat
197	closed subscheme whose ideal is invertible) determines a `T`-point of the
198	Hilbert functor `Hilb_{C/k}(T)` (hence of `Div_{C/k}(T)`), and the dual
199	ideal sheaf `O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` is an invertible sheaf on the
200	relative curve, representing a class in `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)`.
201	
202	The assignment `D ↦ [O_{C ×_k T}(D)]` is functorial in `T` (pullback of an
203	ideal sheaf along `id_C ×_k g : C ×_k T' ⟶ C ×_k T` takes the ideal of `D`
204	to the ideal of `D_{T'}`, since `D` is flat over `T` and hence
205	`D_{T'} = D ×_T T'` is again a relative effective divisor by Kleiman §3
206	Lem. `lm:ctn`).
207	
208	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
209	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
210	
211	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`, sending a
212	relative effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated
213	invertible sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
214	
215	A natural transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`. The source
216	`divFunctor C` is the relative-divisor functor (the open subfunctor of the
217	Hilbert/Quot scheme cut out by the effective-divisor criterion); the target
218	`picSharp C` is the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor.
219	
220	iter-177+: the body constructs the natural transformation component-wise:
221	for each test `T`, send a relative effective Cartier divisor `D` on
222	`C ×_k T` to the dual of its ideal sheaf, then take the étale-sheafified
223	class. Naturality is the functoriality of the dual-of-ideal-sheaf
224	construction under pullback (Stacks 01HG + Kleiman §3 Lem. `lm:ctn`).
225	For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
226	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
227	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
228	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
229	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
230	    divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C :=
231	  sorry
232	
233	/-! ## §3. The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent
234	
235	The structural lemma underlying Step 4 of Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` is the
236	Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma**: given a surjection
237	`α : Z ⟶ P` of étale sheaves with `R := Z ×_P Z` representable, `Z`
238	quasi-projective, and the first projection `R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper,
239	the quotient `P` is representable by a quasi-projective scheme.
240	
241	Kleiman's proof:
242	1. `R ↪ Z ×_k Z` is a flat (from (iv)) and proper monomorphism, hence a
243	   closed immersion by EGA IV 8.11.5.
244	2. By the theory of flat-and-proper effective equivalence relations on
245	   quasi-projective schemes (Altman–Kleiman), there exist a
246	   quasi-projective scheme `Q` and a faithfully flat, projective map
247	   `Z ⟶ Q` with `R = Z ×_Q Z`. The map `Z ⟶ Q` is smooth (its fibers
248	   coincide with those of `R ⟶ Z`, which are smooth).
249	3. In the category of étale sheaves, both `Q` and `P` are coequalisers of
250	   `R ⇒ Z`; uniqueness of the coequaliser gives `Q ≃ P`.
251	
252	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_proper_quotient` (Kleiman §4 Lem.
253	`lm:qt`). -/
254	
255	/-- **The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent of an
256	étale-sheaf surjection.**
257	
258	Let `Z, P : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u` be presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`, and let
259	`α : Z ⟶ P` be a natural transformation. Assume:
260	1. `Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `Y` (encoded by the
261	   `Z.RepresentableBy Y` hypothesis),
262	2. the pullback presheaf `R := Z ×_P Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `R`
263	   (encoded by the `(Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R` hypothesis),
264	3. the first projection `π : R ⟶ Y` (after representation) is smooth and
265	   proper (encoded by the typeclass hypotheses `[Smooth π.left]` and
266	   `[IsProper π.left]`),
267	4. `α` is an étale surjection (encoded as the lift property: every
268	   `T`-point `p ∈ P(T)` lifts to a `T'`-point `z ∈ Z(T')` for some test
269	   morphism `T ⟶ T'`).
270	
271	Then `P` is representable (by a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Q`).
272	
273	iter-177+: the body builds `Q` via the Altman–Kleiman effective quotient
274	of the flat-and-proper equivalence relation `R ↪ Y ×_k Y`, then exhibits
275	the coequaliser-uniqueness in the category of étale sheaves to identify
276	`Q` with the representing object of `P`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton
277	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
278	theorem smoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
279	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
280	    (α : Z ⟶ P)
281	    (Y : Over (Spec (.of k)))
282	    (_hZ : Z.RepresentableBy Y)
283	    (R : Over (Spec (.of k)))
284	    (_hR : (Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R)
285	    (π : R ⟶ Y)
286	    [Smooth π.left] [IsProper π.left]
287	    (_hα : ∀ ⦃T : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ⦄ (p : P.obj T),
288	        ∃ (T' : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ) (e : T ⟶ T') (z : Z.obj T'),
289	          α.app T' z = P.map e p) :
290	    P.IsRepresentable := by
291	  sorry
292	
293	/-! ## §4. The FGA representability theorem
294	
295	Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Picard scheme (Kleiman §4 Thm.
296	`th:main`), specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k`,
297	`X = C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve: the étale-sheafified
298	relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` is representable by a `k`-scheme,
299	separated and locally of finite type, which is a disjoint union of open
300	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes. The representing scheme is the **Picard
301	scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of `PicScheme` above.
302	
303	Blueprint reference: `thm:fga_pic_representability` (Kleiman §4 Thm.
304	`th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch`). -/
305	
306	/-- **FGA representability of the Picard scheme.**
307	
308	Let `k` be a field and `C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
309	over `k`. Then the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`
310	is representable by the Picard scheme `PicScheme C`.
311	
312	iter-177+: the body assembles the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof:
313	1. Stratify `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` by Hilbert polynomial into a disjoint open
314	   cover `(P^φ)_φ`.
315	2. For each `φ`, bound twists by `m`-regularity to reduce to `P^{φ_0}_0`.
316	3. Factor through the Abel map `A_{C/k}` of `abelMap` to obtain
317	   `Z := P^{φ_0}_0 ×_{Pic^♯} Div_{C/k}`, a quasi-projective open of
318	   `Div_{C/k}`.
319	4. Apply `smoothProperQuotient` to the projection `Z ⟶ P^{φ_0}_0` to
320	   represent each `P^{φ_0}_0`; reassemble over `φ` for the full
321	   `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
322	
323	For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
324	noncomputable def representable {k : Type u} [Field k]
325	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
326	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
327	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
328	    (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C) :=
329	  sorry
330	
331	/-! ## §5. The abelian group-scheme structure
332	
333	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` inherits an abelian group-scheme structure
334	from the tensor product of invertible sheaves on `C ×_k T`. The relative
335	Picard presheaf `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` is already an abelian
336	group at every `T` (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
337	functorially in `T`; this lifts to the étale sheafification (sheafification
338	of an abelian-presheaf is an abelian-sheaf, since it commutes with finite
339	products in the value category), and then to a group-scheme structure on
340	the representing scheme by Yoneda.
341	
342	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_is_group_scheme` (Kleiman §2 Def. `df:Pfs` +
343	§4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
344	
345	/-- **The Picard scheme is a `k`-group scheme.**
346	
347	The Picard scheme `PicScheme C` carries a canonical `GrpObj` structure (a
348	group-object structure in `Over (Spec (.of k))`), obtained by Yoneda
349	transport of the abelian-group structure on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)` (sum:
350	`[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`; inverse: `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`; unit: `[O_{C ×_k T}]`).
351	
352	The full abelian-group-scheme refinement is the conjunction of this `GrpObj`
353	instance with the `IsCommMonObj (PicScheme C)` predicate (commutativity of
354	the multiplication), which holds because tensor product of invertible
355	sheaves is symmetric; `IsCommMonObj` is a separate instance threaded on top
356	of `GrpObj` in iter-177+.
357	
358	iter-177+: the body invokes the Yoneda lemma to transport the natural
359	abelian-group structure on `T ↦ picSharp C |_T` to the multiplication /
360	inverse / unit morphisms on `PicScheme C`; the group axioms hold at the
361	representable level functorially in `T`, hence on the representing scheme
362	by Yoneda. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
363	noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
364	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
365	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
366	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
367	    GrpObj (PicScheme C) :=
368	  sorry
369	
370	end PicScheme
371	
372	end Scheme
373	
374	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (iter-176 Lane E file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
19	
20	iter-175 Lane E died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
21	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
22	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the
23	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
24	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a
25	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
26	deep (Nitsure~\S4 ~250-line argument: generic-flatness ⟶ Noetherian
27	induction ⟶ Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity ⟶ assembly via direct images
28	`E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`).
29	
30	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
33	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
34	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
35	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
36	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
37	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
38	   affine-local descent characterisation.
39	
40	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
41	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
42	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
43	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
46	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
47	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
48	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
49	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
50	   stratum.
51	
52	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
53	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
54	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
55	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
56	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
57	
58	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
59	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
60	
61	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
62	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
63	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
64	   strata.
65	
66	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
67	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
68	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
69	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
70	
71	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
72	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
73	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
74	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
75	   stratification.
76	
77	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
78	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
79	   `C ×_k T → T`.
80	
81	## Note on type expressivity
82	
83	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
84	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
85	
86	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
87	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
88	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
89	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
90	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
91	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
92	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
93	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
94	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
95	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
96	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
97	  one trivialises if dropped).
98	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
99	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
100	  bijection content.
101	
102	## Mathlib status
103	
104	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`,
105	`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat` (morphism-level), `IsImmersion`,
106	`IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`. It does NOT
107	provide: a relative projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, an `IsProjective` morphism
108	property, the flattening-stratification construction, or coherent-sheaf
109	flatness as a sheaf-level predicate.
110	
111	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
112	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
113	restriction is harmless in the Route~A consumer setting where `π` comes
114	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
115	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
116	property, the hypothesis tightens.
117

137	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
138	
139	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
140	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
141	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
142	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
143	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
144	
145	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
146	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
147	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
148	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
149	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
152	
153	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
154	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
155	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
156	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
157	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
158	morphism of `f`).
159	
160	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
161	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
162	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
163	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
164	the flattening-stratification theorem.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
167	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
168	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
169	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
170	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
171	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
172	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
175	
176	end Scheme
177	
178	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
179	
180	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
181	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
182	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
183	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
184	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
185	
186	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
187	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
188	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
189	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
190	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
191	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
194	
195	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
196	
197	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
198	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
199	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
200	
201	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
202	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
203	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
204	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
205	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
206	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
207	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
208	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) :
210	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
211	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
212	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
213	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
214	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
215	  sorry
216	
217	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
218	
219	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
220	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
221	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
222	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
223	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
224	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
225	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
226	
227	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
228	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
229	
230	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
231	
232	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
233	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
234	subschemes such that
235	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
236	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
237	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
238	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
239	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
240	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
241	  scope).
242	
243	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
244	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
245	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
246	
247	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
248	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
249	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
250	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
251	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
252	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
253	    (F : S.Modules) :
254	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
255	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
256	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
257	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
258	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
259	  sorry
260	
261	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
262	case opening]
263	
264	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
265	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
266	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
267	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
268	
269	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
270	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
271	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
272	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
273	Noetherianity).
274	
275	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
276	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
277	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
278	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
279	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
280	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
281	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
282	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
283	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
284	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
285	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
286	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
287	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
288	  sorry
289	
290	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
291	
292	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
293	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
294	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
295	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
296	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
297	
298	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
299	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
300	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
301	
302	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
303	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
304	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
305	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
306	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
307	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
308	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
309	iter-177+ work. -/
310	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
311	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
312	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
313	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
314	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
315	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
316	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
317	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
318	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
319	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
320	  sorry
321	
322	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
323	
324	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
325	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
326	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
327	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
328	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
329	
330	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
331	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
332	
333	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
334	Stacks 052H].
335	
336	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
337	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
338	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
339	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
340	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
341	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
342	
343	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
344	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
345	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
346	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
347	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
348	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
349	type-clarity.
350	
351	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
352	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
353	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
354	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
355	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
356	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
357	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
358	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
359	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
360	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
361	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
362	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
363	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
365	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
366	  sorry
367	
368	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
369	
370	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
371	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
372	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
373	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
374	
375	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
376	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
377	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
378	Nitsure's part (ii).
379	
380	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
381	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
382	
383	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
384	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
385	
386	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
387	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
388	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
389	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
390	
391	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
392	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
393	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
394	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
395	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
396	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
397	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
398	available. -/
399	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
400	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
401	    (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
402	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
403	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
404	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
405	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
406	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
407	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
408	  sorry
409	
410	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
411	
412	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
413	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
414	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
415	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
416	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
417	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
418	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
419	explicitly. -/
420	
421	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
422	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
423	
424	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
425	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
426	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
427	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
428	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
429	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
430	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
431	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
432	
433	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
434	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
435	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
436	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
437	typed `sorry`. -/
438	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
439	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
440	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
442	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) :
443	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
444	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
445	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
446	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
447	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
448	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
449	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
450	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) := by
451	  sorry
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)

11	This file is the **A.1.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the line-bundle
13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22

24	
25	This file is the **iter-174 Lane E** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned
26	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
27	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex`) with a
28	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters
29	`Picard_RelativeSpec.lean` (A.1.a) settles its `QcohAlgebra` body, and after

48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.
