88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). The iter-174 file-skeleton therefore
96	encodes the type of line bundles on the product by a typed `sorry` at the type
97	level; iter-175+ will instantiate it as a subtype of `(pullback πC πT).Modules`
98	cutting out invertibility.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
104	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
105	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
106	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
107	Picard functor at `T`.
108	
109	iter-175+: instantiate as a subtype of `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrying an
110	invertibility witness — concretely either
111	```
112	structure OnProduct ... where
113	  sheaf : (pullback πC πT).Modules
114	  isInvertible : IsInvertible sheaf
115	```
116	or the quotient by isomorphism for the Picard *group* refinement. For the
117	iter-174 file-skeleton this is a typed `sorry` at the type level because
118	Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `IsInvertible` predicate on `Scheme.Modules`. -/
119	noncomputable def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (_πC : C ⟶ S) (_πT : T ⟶ S) :
120	    Type (u+1) :=
121	  sorry
122	
123	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
124	
125	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
126	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
127	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
128	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
129	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
130	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
131	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
132	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
133	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
136	01HH). -/
137	
138	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
139	curve. Sends an `O_T`-line bundle `N` to the `O_(C ×_S T)`-line bundle
140	`π_T^* N`, induced by the underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on
141	`Scheme.Modules`.
142	
143	iter-175+: instantiate as `(Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N`
144	wrapped with the carried `IsInvertible` witness (the pullback of an invertible
145	sheaf is invertible, Stacks 01HH). For the iter-174 file-skeleton the body is
146	a typed `sorry` because the carrier type `OnProduct` is itself a typed `sorry`
147	(see §1). The signature is non-tautological: it asserts a *function*
148	`T.Modules → OnProduct πC πT`, which after unfolding `OnProduct` becomes the
149	concrete pullback functor restricted to invertible modules. -/
150	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
151	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (_N : T.Modules) : OnProduct πC πT :=
152	  sorry
153	
154	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
155	
156	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
157	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
158	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
159	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
160	
161	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
162	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
163	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
164	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
165	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
166	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
167	
168	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
169	
170	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
171	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
172	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
173	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
174	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
175	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
176	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
177	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
178	```
179	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
180	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
181	       │                                                              │
182	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
183	       ▼                                                              ▼
184	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
185	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
186	```
187	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
188	```
189	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
190	  ≅
191	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
192	```
193	
194	This natural isomorphism is the substantive content of the
195	"line-bundle pullback functor is well-behaved under composition" fact
196	(Stacks tag 01HG). At the level of the `OnProduct` carrier (which is a typed
197	`sorry` in iter-174; see §1) it descends to equality of isomorphism classes
198	`[g_C^* π_T^* N] = [π_{T'}^* g^* N] ∈ OnProduct πC πT'`.
199	
200	iter-175+: the body is `Modules.pullbackComp` composed twice (once on each
201	route) and patched together by the equation `πT' = g ≫ πT` (which gives
202	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` from the pullback square). For the iter-174
203	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
204	theorem pullback_pullback_eq {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
205	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
206	    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (N : T.Modules) :
207	    Nonempty
208	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
209	            (Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
210	              (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg]))).obj
211	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N) ≅
212	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT')).obj
213	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N)) := by
214	  sorry
215	
216	end LineBundle
217	
218	namespace RelPicPresheaf
219	
220	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
221	
222	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
223	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
224	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
225	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
226	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
227	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
228	Picard presheaf at `T`.
229	
230	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
231	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
232	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
233	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
234	
235	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
236	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
237	
238	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
239	
240	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
241	presheaf
242	```
243	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
244	```
245	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
246	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
247	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
248	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
249	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
250	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
251	
252	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
253	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
254	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
255	
256	iter-175+: the body constructs the `Setoid` from the explicit relation `L ~ L'
257	↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅ pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N`. For the
258	iter-174 file-skeleton this is a typed `sorry` because `OnProduct` itself is a
259	typed `sorry` (so the tensor product `L ⊗ L'⁻¹` on the carrier is not yet
260	typed). -/
261	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
262	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
263	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) :=
264	  sorry
265	
266	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
267	
268	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
269	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
270	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
271	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
272	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
273	on quotients
274	```
275	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
276	```
277	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
278	
279	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
280	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
281	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
282	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
283	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
284	
285	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
286	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
287	
288	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
289	
290	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
291	is a canonical map
292	```
293	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
294	```
295	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
296	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
297	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
298	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
299	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
300	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
301	
302	iter-175+: the body builds the map via `Quotient.lift` on the lifted `g_C^*`,
303	using the well-definedness from `pullback_pullback_eq`; the identity and
304	composition laws (giving the full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging) are
305	the lemmas `id_eq` and `comp_eq` of the quotient-induced map (immediate from
306	`Modules.pullbackId` and `Modules.pullbackComp`). For the iter-174 file-skeleton
307	the body is a typed `sorry`. The signature is non-tautological: it asserts a
308	map between two distinct quotient sets that depends on the morphism `g`. -/
309	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
310	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (_hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
311	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
312	  sorry
313	
314	end RelPicPresheaf
315	
316	end Scheme
317	
318	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).
19	
20	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
21	
22	iter-175 Lane H died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
23	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
24	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations
25	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
26	`\lean{...}` pin in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`) with a
27	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
28	deep (Nitsure §5: boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening
29	stratification ⟶ valuative criterion).
30	
31	The 6 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
34	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
35	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
36	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
37	
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def, ~6 LOC) — the **Quot
39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
48	
49	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
53	
54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
59	
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).
89	
90	## Mathlib status
91	
92	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
94	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
95	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
96	- `CategoryTheory.IsPullback` for cartesian squares,
97	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
98	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiCompact`,
99	  `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiSeparated`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`,
100	  `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
101	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
102	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
103	
104	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
105	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
106	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
107	- a `IsProjective` morphism property,
108	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
109	- `R^i f_*` higher direct images on `Scheme.Modules`,
110	- Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity,
111	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
112	
113	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
114	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
115	restriction is harmless in the Route A consumer setting where `π` comes
116	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
117	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
118	property, the hypothesis tightens.

139	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
140	
141	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
142	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
143	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
144	polynomial is the function
145	
146	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
147	
148	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
149	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
152	III.5.2). -/
153	
154	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
155	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
156	
157	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
158	
159	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
160	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
161	
162	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
163	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
164	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
167	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
168	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
169	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
170	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
171	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
172	    Polynomial ℚ :=
173	  sorry
174	
175	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
176	
177	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
178	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
179	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
180	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
181	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
182	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
183	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
184	
185	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
186	
187	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
188	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
191	
192	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
193	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
194	
195	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
196	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
197	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
198	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
199	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
200	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
201	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
202	
203	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
204	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
205	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
206	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
207	is a typed `sorry`. -/
208	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
210	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
211	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=

216	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
217	
218	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
219	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
220	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
221	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
222	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
223	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
224	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
225	
226	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
227	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
228	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
229	
230	namespace Scheme
231	
232	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
233	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
234	
235	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
236	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
237	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
238	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
239	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
240	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
241	
242	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
243	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
244	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
245	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
246	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
247	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
248	  sorry
249	
250	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
251	
252	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
253	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
254	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
255	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
256	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
257	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
258	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
259	
260	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
261	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
262	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
263	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
264	work (once the proof body lands).
265	
266	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
267	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
268	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
269	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
270	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
271	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
272	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
273	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
274	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
275	  sorry

277	/-! ## §4. Representability of the Quot scheme
278	
279	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman: for a noetherian `S`, a projective
280	`π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent
281	`E` on `X`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
282	representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.
283	
284	The proof has four steps (Nitsure §5):
285	1. **Boundedness** via Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity (uniform across
286	   fibers of `π` and across coherent quotients of `E_s` with Hilbert
287	   polynomial `Φ`).
288	2. **Grassmannian embedding**
289	   `α : Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} ↪ Grass(W ⊗_{O_S} Sym^r V, Φ(r))`
290	   for `r ≥ m`, sending `⟨F, q⟩ ↦ ⟨(π_T)_* F(r), (π_T)_*(q(r))⟩`.
291	3. **Locally closed in Grassmannian** via the flattening stratification
292	   applied to the universal cokernel on the Grassmannian, producing the
293	   stratum `T_0^Φ`.
294	4. **Closed embedding** by the valuative criterion of properness for
295	   DVRs.
296	
297	The reduction to the universal case `X = ℙ(V)`, `E = π*W` is recorded as
298	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W` in the blueprint chapter.
299	
300	Blueprint reference: `thm:quot_representable` (Nitsure §5; FGA Explained
301	Ch. 5; Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV). -/
302	
303	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman).
304	
305	Let `S` be a noetherian scheme, `π : X ⟶ S` a projective morphism (here
306	encoded as a proper `LocallyOfFiniteType` morphism; the projectivity
307	upgrades once `IsProjective` lands in Mathlib), `L` a line bundle on `X`
308	(relatively very ample), `E` a coherent `O_X`-module, and
309	`Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`. Then the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `QuotFunctor`
310	is representable by an `S`-scheme.
311	
312	We package the conclusion as the existence of `Q : Over S` together with
313	a `Functor.RepresentableBy Q` witness for `QuotFunctor π L E Φ`; the
314	*projectivity* of `Q ⟶ S` (and the universal quotient
315	`q^univ : π^*_Q E ↠ F^univ` on `X ×_S Q^{Φ,L}`) is implicit in the
316	construction (Plücker-embedded into a projective Grassmannian over `S`)
317	and is iter-177+ refinement work.
318	
319	iter-177+: the body follows the four-step Nitsure §5 proof
320	(boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening stratification ⟶
321	valuative-criterion closed embedding); the sub-lemmas live in
322	`lem:quot_boundedness`, `lem:quot_alpha_injective`,
323	`lem:quot_valuative_criterion`, and the existential reduction in
324	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the
325	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
326	theorem QuotScheme {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
327	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
328	    (L E : X.Modules) (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
329	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by
330	  sorry
331	
332	end Scheme

334	/-! ## §5. Cohomology and base change
335	
336	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
337	boundedness step (Nitsure §5 "Use of m-Regularity") and the Grassmannian
338	embedding (Nitsure §5 "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"). We record the
339	relevant statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it
340	directly.
341	
342	The Stacks 02KH form is the statement for higher direct images
343	`R^i f_*` on quasi-coherent sheaves; for the iter-176 file-skeleton we
344	state the `i = 0` form on `Scheme.Modules`, which is the substantive
345	content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of Stacks 02KH. The
346	`R^i` form is iter-177+ work after higher-direct-image infrastructure
347	is in scope.
348	
349	Blueprint reference: `thm:flat_base_change_cohomology` (Stacks 02KH). -/
350	
351	/-! ### Flat base change of cohomology (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0` form)
352	
353	Let
354	```
355	   g'
356	X' ───→ X
357	│       │
358	f'      f
359	↓       ↓
360	S' ───→ S
361	   g
362	```
363	be a cartesian square of schemes with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact
364	quasi-separated. Let `F` be a sheaf of `O_X`-modules. Then the canonical
365	base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an isomorphism in
366	`S'.Modules`.
367	
368	(The full Stacks 02KH statement covers all higher direct images
369	`R^i f_* F` for `i ≥ 0`; the `i = 0` form encoded here is the
370	substantive content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of
371	Stacks 02KH, with the `i ≥ 1` form post-iter-177 work after the
372	higher-direct-image bifunctor lands.)
373	
374	iter-177 (Lane QS-FLAT): the body constructs the canonical base-change
375	natural transformation via the mate equivalence of the
376	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction (Mathlib's `mateEquiv` of
377	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`), then exhibits the iso
378	via the `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` helper. The deep mathematical
379	content (Stacks tag 02KH / 02KE / 00H8) lives entirely in the helper;
380	it reduces affine-locally to: for a flat ring map `A → B` and an
381	`A`-algebra `R`, the canonical map `B ⊗_A H^i(X, F) → H^i(X_B, F_B)`
382	is an iso for any quasi-coherent `F` (the `i = 0` form is what we use).
383	The helper remains a typed `sorry` pending the affine-local reduction
384	+ algebraic flat base change; this is iter-178+ body work after
385	quasi-compact open-cover Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure is in scope. -/
386	
387	/-- The canonical base-change natural transformation `g* (f_* -) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* -)`
388	associated to a cartesian square
389	```
390	     g'
391	X' ─────→ X
392	│         │
393	f'        f
394	↓         ↓
395	S' ─────→ S
396	     g
397	```
398	in `Scheme`. Constructed as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform)
399	under the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions on sheaves of modules
400	of the canonical 2-isomorphism
401	`pullback g ⋙ pullback f' ≅ pullback f ⋙ pullback g'` coming from
402	`g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`.
403	
404	This natural transformation always exists (no flatness needed). The
405	content of the flat base-change theorem (Stacks tag 02KH) is the
406	*isomorphism* claim under the hypotheses
407	`[QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]`; that claim is the
408	helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`. -/
409	noncomputable def canonicalBaseChangeMap
410	    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
411	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
412	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) :
413	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback g ⟶
414	      Scheme.Modules.pullback g' ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f' :=
415	  CategoryTheory.mateEquiv
416	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
417	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f')
418	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f' g) ≪≫
419	        Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr sq.w.symm ≪≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g' f).symm).hom)
421	
422	/-- **Section-wise form of flat base change** (Stacks tag 02KH(ii)).
423	
424	For every open `U` of `S'`, the section over `U` of the canonical base-change
425	map `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶ (pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)`
426	is an isomorphism.
427	
428	This is the substantive content of Stacks 02KH(ii) (the `i = 0` form):
429	* on quasi-compact opens `U ⊆ S'` it reduces to the algebraic flat base
430	  change `B ⊗_A H⁰(X, F) ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)` (Stacks 00H8 / 02KE), via
431	  `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on the ring map `A = Γ(S, V) → B = Γ(S', V')`
432	  for an affine open `V ⊆ S` with `V' = g⁻¹V`;
433	* the general case follows by Mayer-Vietoris from the quasi-separation of
434	  `f` (so intersections of preimages are quasi-compact, hence the algebraic
435	  step applies to each pair of affine charts).
436	
437	iter-178+: the body of this helper is the deep mathematical content of
438	flat base change of cohomology. It requires both
439	(i) the affine-local identification of `Scheme.Modules` sections of
440	    `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` over an affine open `U`
441	    with the algebraic tensor product `Γ(S', U) ⊗_{Γ(S, V)} Γ(X_V, F|_{X_V})`,
442	    and
443	(ii) an open-cover Mayer-Vietoris on `S'.Modules` sections.
444	Neither piece is in scope at the pinned Mathlib commit. The body is a
445	typed `sorry` carrying the *intended* mathematical content
446	(Stacks 02KH(ii)). -/
447	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
448	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
449	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
450	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
451	    (F : X.Modules) (U : S'.Opens) :
452	    IsIso (((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F).app U) := by
453	  -- Stacks 02KH(ii) (i = 0, section form). Proof strategy (iter-178+):
454	  --   1. Pick an affine open `V ⊆ S` together with a quasi-compact open
455	  --      `V' = g⁻¹V ⊆ S'`. On `V'` the section of
456	  --      `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` identifies with
457	  --      `Γ(S', V') ⊗_{Γ(S, V)} Γ(X, f⁻¹V, F)`.
458	  --   2. The section of `(pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)` on `V'`
459	  --      identifies with `Γ(X', (f')⁻¹V', F')` where `F' = (g')* F`.
460	  --   3. The algebraic flat base change `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on the
461	  --      ring map `Γ(S, V) → Γ(S', V')` (flat by `Flat g`) gives the iso
462	  --      on the affine open `V'`.
463	  --   4. Quasi-separated `f` ⟹ `f⁻¹V ∩ f⁻¹V₀` is quasi-compact for any
464	  --      pair of affine opens, so Mayer-Vietoris glues the affine-local
465	  --      iso to an iso on the whole `U`.
466	  -- Pending: project-side sub-build identifying `Scheme.Modules` sections
467	  -- with explicit tensor products of `Γ(X, F)` modules, plus an
468	  -- open-cover Mayer-Vietoris on quasi-compact quasi-separated targets.
469	  sorry
470	
471	/-- **Flat base-change is an isomorphism** (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0`).
472	
473	The canonical base-change natural transformation `canonicalBaseChangeMap`
474	is an isomorphism at every coherent sheaf `F` under the hypotheses
475	`[QuasiCompact f]`, `[QuasiSeparated f]`, `[Flat g]`.
476	
477	The proof reduces section-wise via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
478	to the section-form helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso`,
479	which captures Stacks 02KH(ii) — the substantive algebraic content
480	(`Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on each affine open + Mayer-Vietoris for
481	quasi-separated `f`). -/
482	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
483	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
484	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
485	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
486	    (F : X.Modules) :
487	    IsIso ((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F) :=
488	  Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mpr
489	    (fun U => canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso sq F U)
490	
491	theorem flatBaseChangeCohomology {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
492	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
493	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
494	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
495	    (F : X.Modules) :
496	    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj
497	                ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj F) ≅
498	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj
499	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)) :=
500	  -- Build the canonical Beck-Chevalley base-change map and wrap it in `asIso`
501	  -- using the iso-claim from `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`.
502	  ⟨@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso sq F)⟩
503	
504	end AlgebraicGeometry

16	`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean` (A.1.b) to its

82	## Note on type expressivity
83	
84	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
85	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
86	
87	- `addCommGroup` is an `AddCommGroup` instance on a quotient set whose
88	  underlying type is itself a typed `sorry` in A.1.b (`OnProduct` carrier);
89	  the substantive content is the existence of the group structure
90	  (closure under `+`, inverse, neutral element), which is non-trivial
91	  even at the level of typed-`sorry` carriers — once the carrier is
92	  unpacked, the group operations are induced by the tensor product on
93	  invertible sheaves modulo the subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
94	
95	- `PicSharp` returns a contravariant *functor*, not a constant family;
96	  the object and morphism actions are independently substantive (the
97	  morphism action is the set-valued `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` of
98	  A.1.b lifted to a group homomorphism).
99	
100	- `PicSharp.functorial` returns an `AddMonoidHom`, not a bare set map;
101	  this is a strict strengthening of the set-valued version in A.1.b.
102	
103	- `PicSharp.presheaf` returns a `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`,
104	  which on objects matches `PicSharp` and on morphisms matches
105	  `PicSharp.functorial` — substantive content is the assembly into a
106	  single category-theoretic object.
107	
108	- `PicSharp.etSheaf` returns a `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` for the given
109	  topology `J`; the sheafification is non-trivial because `PicSharp`
110	  is generally not even a Zariski sheaf (Kleiman §2, L1292–L1302).
111

163	/-! ## §1. Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient
164	
165	The Picard group `Pic(X)` of any scheme is canonically an abelian group
166	under tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of
167	`[L]` is `[L⁻¹] = [Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)]`). The pullback map
168	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_k T)` of
169	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection` (A.1.b)
170	respects this structure: it sends `O_T ↦ O_{C ×_k T}` (the structure
171	sheaf is preserved by inverse image) and is multiplicative on tensor
172	products (Stacks 01HH for invertibility, Mathlib's
173	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` for the underlying multiplicativity).
174	
175	Therefore `π_T^* Pic(T)` is a subgroup of `Pic(C ×_k T)`, and the
176	quotient `Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` inherits a canonical
177	abelian-group structure under which the quotient map is a surjective
178	homomorphism with kernel exactly `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
179	
180	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Kleiman §2,
181	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01CR). -/
182	
183	/-- **Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient.**
184	
185	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, and a test
186	object `πT : T ⟶ S`, the quotient set
187	```
188	Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)
189	  = Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
190	```
191	carries a canonical abelian-group structure: addition is the descent of
192	tensor product `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']`, the zero element is the class
193	`[O_{C ×_S T}]`, and the inverse is `-[L] := [L⁻¹]` (the dual line
194	bundle). Well-definedness on the quotient uses the subgroup property of
195	`π_T^* Pic(T)` from `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid`.
196	
197	iter-177+: the body builds the `AddCommGroup` instance via
198	Mathlib's `QuotientAddGroup` on the abelian group `Pic(C ×_S T)` (tensor
199	product) modulo the subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)` (image of a group hom).
200	For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry` because the
201	underlying carrier `LineBundle.OnProduct` is itself a typed `sorry` in
202	A.1.b (so no concrete tensor-product law is yet available); the
203	signature still asserts the substantive group instance on the named
204	quotient set. -/
205	noncomputable instance addCommGroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
206	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
207	    AddCommGroup (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) := by
208	  -- iter-177 Lane RPF — gated on A.1.b `LineBundle.OnProduct` typed sorry.
209	  --
210	  -- Intended construction once A.1.b lands:
211	  --   1. `LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT` carries `AddCommGroup` (tensor product
212	  --      on invertible sheaves; Stacks tag 01CR, Hartshorne II §6: unit is the
213	  --      structure sheaf `O_{C×_S T}`, inverse is the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O)`).
214	  --   2. `pullbackHom : T.LineBundle →+ LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT` is the
215	  --      additive structure underlying `LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT`
216	  --      (preserves zero: `π_T^* O_T ≅ O_{C×_S T}`; preserves addition: tensor
217	  --      compatibility of pullback `π_T^*(N ⊗ N') ≅ π_T^* N ⊗ π_T^* N'`).
218	  --      Let `H := pullbackHom.range : AddSubgroup (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)`.
219	  --   3. The Setoid `preimage_subgroup πC πT` coincides with
220	  --      `QuotientAddGroup.leftRel H` (both encode `L ~ L' ↔ L⁻¹ ⊗ L' ∈ H`),
221	  --      hence `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) ≃ LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT ⧸ H`.
222	  --   4. The RHS has the canonical `AddCommGroup` from
223	  --      `QuotientAddGroup.Quotient.addCommGroup`; transport along the Equiv of
224	  --      (3) via `Equiv.addCommGroup` (Mathlib.Algebra.Group.TransferInstance)
225	  --      yields the desired instance on the LHS.
226	  --
227	  -- Steps (1)-(3) require `LineBundle.OnProduct` to be unpacked (currently a
228	  -- typed `sorry` at `Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean` L119). Step (4) is the
229	  -- project-side closure: a one-liner once (3) is in hand.
230	  --
231	  -- TODO (A.1.b gate): close once `LineBundle.OnProduct` is upgraded from a
232	  -- typed `sorry` to a concrete structure carrying invertible-sheaf tensor
233	  -- product, and `RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` is upgraded from a typed
234	  -- `sorry` to `QuotientAddGroup.leftRel (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection ...).range`.
235	  exact sorry
236	
237	end PicSharp

239	/-! ## §2. The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued functor
240	
241	We assemble the data of A.1.b (object-level quotient set,
242	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial` morphism action) and §1 (abelian-group
243	instance on each quotient) into a single contravariant functor
244	
245	```
246	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
247	```
248	
249	sending an `Spec k`-scheme `T` to the abelian group
250	`Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (with the structure of §1), and a
251	morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `Spec k` to the group homomorphism
252	`g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/k}(T')` descended from the
253	line-bundle pullback `g_C^* := (id_C ×_k g)^*`.
254	
255	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_sharp` (Kleiman §2, Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
256	
257	/-- The **relative Picard functor** of a smooth proper geometrically
258	integral curve `C` over a field `k`, as a contravariant functor
259	
260	```
261	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
262	```
263	
264	On objects: `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, the abelian-group
265	quotient of `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Lean instance
266	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`).
267	
268	On morphisms: `g ↦ g^♯`, the group homomorphism descended from
269	`g_C^* = (id_C ×_k g)^*` via the quotient (the set-level map is
270	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial`; the group-hom upgrade is
271	`PicSharp.functorial` below).
272	
273	Universe: object map values are `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` because the
274	underlying carrier `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` lives in
275	`Type (u+1)` (since `LineBundle.OnProduct` is defined to land in
276	`Type (u+1)`).
277	
278	iter-177+: the body builds the functor explicitly from
279	`AddCommGrpCat.of (Quotient (preimage_subgroup C.hom T.hom))` on
280	objects and from `PicSharp.functorial` on morphisms. The identity and
281	composition laws follow from `Scheme.Modules.pullbackId` /
282	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp` descended through the quotient. For the
283	iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
284	noncomputable def PicSharp {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
285	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
286	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
287	  sorry
288	
289	namespace PicSharp
290	
291	/-! ## §3. Functoriality (group-homomorphism strengthening)
292	
293	The naturality lemma `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` of A.1.b produces, for
294	each morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, a set map
295	```
296	g^♯ : Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T) ⟶ Pic(C ×_S T') / π_{T'}^* Pic(T')
297	```
298	of quotient sets. Combined with the abelian-group instance of §1 on
299	both sides, this set map upgrades to an additive-monoid homomorphism
300	`AddMonoidHom`: indeed `g_C^*` preserves tensor products and the
301	structure sheaf, so it preserves the abelian-group operations on both
302	sides; the upgrade is the substantive content of
303	`lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial`.
304	
305	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial` (Kleiman §2,
306	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/
307	
308	/-- **Functoriality of the relative Picard presheaf, group-hom form.**
309	
310	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects
311	`πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
312	(encoded by `πT' = g ≫ πT`), the set map
313	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial πC πT πT' g hg` upgrades to an
314	`AddMonoidHom`-homomorphism with respect to the abelian-group structure
315	of `PicSharp.addCommGroup` on source and target.
316	
317	iter-177+: the body builds the `AddMonoidHom` from the set map by
318	verifying `map_zero` (the image of `[O_{C ×_S T}]` is `[O_{C ×_S T'}]`,
319	i.e. pullback of the structure sheaf is the structure sheaf) and
320	`map_add` (pullback preserves tensor products: Stacks 01HG /
321	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensor`). For the iter-176 file-skeleton the
322	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
323	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
324	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
325	    (_hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
326	    Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →+
327	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
328	  sorry
329	
330	/-! ## §4. Wrapping as a functor instance
331	
332	The group-valued presheaf bundling: combine the on-objects assignment
333	of `PicSharp` (carrying the `addCommGroup` structure) with the
334	on-morphisms assignment of `functorial` (each a group hom) into a
335	single functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`. The identity /
336	composition laws of the functor are inherited from the corresponding
337	identities on `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, descended through the
338	quotient.
339	
340	This wrapper is kept distinct from `PicSharp` to mirror the blueprint
341	split (`def:rel_pic_sharp` vs.\ `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`) and to
342	record the explicit re-packaging step from the lemma-by-lemma data
343	(`addCommGroup`, `PicSharp`, `functorial`) into a single
344	category-theoretic functor — useful when applying functorial
345	constructions (Yoneda, sheafification, …) to the *bundled* form.
346	
347	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` (Kleiman §2,
348	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/
349	
350	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf, bundled.**
351	
352	The relative Picard functor `PicSharp_{C/k}` packaged as a single
353	contravariant functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, with object
354	action `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (group structure
355	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`) and morphism action `g ↦ g^♯` (group hom from
356	`PicSharp.functorial`). Identity and composition laws are inherited
357	from `Scheme.Modules.pullbackId` / `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`
358	descended through the quotient.
359	
360	iter-177+: the body either re-exports `PicSharp` (if the
361	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued functor is built directly in §2) or constructs
362	the functor from the on-objects/on-morphisms data using the
363	`CategoryTheory.Functor.mk` builder; the body is a typed `sorry` for
364	the iter-176 file-skeleton.
365	
366	Note on naming: this is the canonical "fully assembled" form mentioned
367	as `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` in the blueprint; it is structurally a
368	`def` (not a `theorem`) because the conclusion is a `Functor` (data),
369	not a `Prop`. -/
370	noncomputable def presheaf {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
371	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
372	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
373	  sorry
374	
375	end PicSharp

377	/-! ## §5. Étale sheafification
378	
379	The relative Picard presheaf `PicSharp_{C/k}` is generally not even a
380	Zariski sheaf (Kleiman §2 L1292–L1302). To obtain a representable
381	functor in the sense of `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`
382	(downstream A.2.c), one replaces `PicSharp_{C/k}` by its **associated
383	étale sheaf** `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{∼ét}`. Kleiman §4
384	(Theorem `th:main`) represents precisely this sheafified functor.
385	
386	For the iter-176 file-skeleton, we encode the sheafification as
387	`presheafToSheaf J _` applied to the bundled `PicSharp.presheaf` for a
388	**parameter** Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` — because
389	Mathlib at the pinned commit does not ship an étale Grothendieck
390	topology on schemes (it ships only the morphism property
391	`AlgebraicGeometry.Etale`). iter-177+ refinement: bind `J` to the
392	canonical étale topology once it lands in Mathlib.
393	
394	*Naming note*: the blueprint pins this declaration under
395	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`, which already names the
396	*representing scheme* in `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`. We use
397	the namespaced name `PicSharp.etSheaf` here; the blueprint will be
398	updated by the plan/review agents.
399	
400	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification` (Kleiman §2,
401	`df:Pfs` étale-sheaf clause). -/
402	
403	/-- The **étale sheafification of the relative Picard presheaf**.
404	
405	Given a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C` over a field
406	`k`, and a Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` (intended: the
407	canonical étale topology on `(Sch/k)`), the **étale-sheafified relative
408	Picard functor**
409	
410	```
411	Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{~_ét}
412	```
413	
414	is the sheafification of the group-valued presheaf
415	`PicSharp.presheaf` of `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`. Equivalently, it is
416	the unique (up to canonical isomorphism) sheaf of abelian groups on the
417	site `(Over (Spec k), J)` equipped with a presheaf morphism
418	`PicSharp.presheaf ⟶ (forget _) ∘ -` universal among presheaf morphisms
419	to abelian-group sheaves.
420	
421	Encoded as a `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` object; iter-177+ implementations
422	will fix `J` to the étale topology and unfold via
423	`CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf` (Mathlib's
424	`Sites.ConcreteSheafification`). The body of the iter-176 file-skeleton
425	is a typed `sorry`.
426	
427	In Kleiman's notation with `X = C` and `S = Spec k`, this is
428	`Pic_{(X/S)ét}` from `df:Pfs`. -/
429	noncomputable def PicSharp.etSheaf {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
430	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom]
431	    (J : GrothendieckTopology (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :
432	    Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
433	  sorry
434	
435	namespace PicSharp
436	
437	/-! ## §6. Sheafification preserves the abelian-group structure
438	
439	The étale sheafification of an abelian-group-valued presheaf is itself
440	abelian-group-valued: the forgetful functor `AddCommGrpCat ⥤ Type`
441	preserves filtered colimits (these are computed pointwise), and the
442	plus-construction is built from filtered colimits, so sheafification
443	commutes with the forgetful functor up to canonical isomorphism. The
444	substantive content is the **sheafification unit**: a canonical
445	morphism of presheaves `PicSharp.presheaf ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf ?).obj`
446	(in `AddCommGrpCat`) that exhibits the universal property of
447	sheafification.
448	
449	This statement does NOT have an explicit `\lean{...}` pin in the
450	blueprint (`thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` is a description
451	rather than a Lean target); we surface it here so the downstream
452	representability statement (`chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`) can
453	cite the unit directly.
454	
455	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` (Kleiman
456	§2, `df:Pfs`; standard `Sheafification.toSheafify` content). -/
457	
458	/-- **Sheafification unit for the étale Picard sheafification.**
459	
460	Given the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor
461	`PicSharp.etSheaf C J : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat`, there is a canonical
462	morphism of (group-valued) presheaves
463	```
464	η_C : PicSharp.presheaf C ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj
465	```
466	in the functor category `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`. This
467	morphism is the **unit of sheafification**: it is universal among
468	morphisms from `PicSharp.presheaf C` to any abelian-group-valued
469	étale sheaf.
470	
471	iter-177+: the body returns
472	`(CategoryTheory.toSheafify J (PicSharp.presheaf C))` (using Mathlib's
473	`Sites.ConcreteSheafification` API) — assuming `J` admits abelian-group
474	sheafification, which is provided by `HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat`
475	(supplied by Mathlib for the relevant universes via its
476	abelian-category-target sheafification). For the iter-176 file-skeleton
477	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
478	theorem etSheafUnit {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
479	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
480	    (J : GrothendieckTopology (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :
481	    Nonempty (PicSharp.presheaf C ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj) := by
482	  sorry
483	
484	end PicSharp
485	
486	end Scheme
487	
488	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point and its global sections (RR.3)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.3** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)` and the
22	  degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and the Riemann–Roch dimension formula
25	  `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0`.
26	- **`RR.3` (this file)**: the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point
27	  `P ∈ C`, its `k̄`-vector space of global sections as the
28	  Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`, the `H¹`-vanishing
29	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a genus-`0` curve, the dimension formula
30	  `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`, and the existence of a non-constant
31	  rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with at most a simple pole at `P`.
32	- `RR.4` (`RationalCurveIso.lean`, future): the "two-section
33	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
34	
35	## Status (iter-176 Lane K file-skeleton)
36	
37	This file is the **iter-176 Lane K** file-skeleton (a re-dispatch of the
38	iter-175 Lane K casualty that died to the Anthropic session-limit reset
39	window before the file was ever created). Each of the five pinned
40	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching
41	the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_OCofP.tex`) with a
42	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work after the sibling chapters
43	`RR.1`'s `RationalMap.order` body lands (iter-176 Lane D target) and the
44	`RR.2` cohomology-LES API stabilises.
45	
46	The 5 pinned declarations are:
47	
48	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint` — the invertible
49	   sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` associated to a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
50	   curve `C / k̄`.
51	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff`
52	   — the identification of global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` with the
53	   Riemann–Roch space
54	   `L([P]) = {f ∈ K(C)^× | div(f) + [P] ≥ 0} ∪ {0}`,
55	   expressed as an `Iff`-style characterisation of the order conditions
56	   `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
57	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.h1_vanishing_genusZero`
58	   — the cohomological vanishing `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a smooth proper
59	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, via the long
60	   exact sequence of the closed-point short exact sequence
61	   `0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`.
62	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
63	   — the dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus `0`,
64	   specialising the χ-identity `RR.2` to `D = [P]` and consuming the
65	   `H¹`-vanishing of pin 3.
66	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists_nonconstant_genusZero`
67	   — the corollary: a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` regular
68	   on `C \ {P}` with at most a simple pole at `P`, obtained as a lift of
69	   any non-zero element of the quotient
70	   `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) / 𝓀̄ · 1`.
71

102	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
103	
104	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
105	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
106	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
107	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
108	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
109	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
110	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
111	
112	namespace Scheme
113	
114	/-- **The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
115	curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6, p. 144, Proposition 6.13(a)).
116	
117	The invertible sheaf cut out (in the dual / `𝒦_C`-subsheaf packaging of
118	Hartshorne `ℒ(D)`) by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on the complement,
119	where `f_P ∈ 𝔪_P ∖ 𝔪_P²` is any uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C,P}`. The
120	result is independent of the choice of uniformiser (two uniformisers
121	differ by a unit) and is an invertible `𝒪_C`-module of rank one.
122	
123	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
124	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf

126	that `H⁰` and `H¹` of `𝒪_C(P)` are accessible via
127	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 0/1`).
128	
129	iter-177+ body: glue the inverse-of-ideal-sheaf construction. Concretely,

136	`HModule` adjunction's left adjoint.

159	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
160	
161	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
162	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
163	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
164	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
165	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
166	
167	/-- **Global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` as rational functions with controlled
168	pole at `P`** (Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7, p. 157).
169	
170	For a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×`, the following are
171	equivalent:
172	
173	* `f` defines a global section of `𝒪_C(P)` (equivalently: there exists
174	  a nonzero `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical
175	  inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` is `f`);
176	* the order conditions hold: `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
177	  `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose generic point is not `P`, and
178	  `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (where the latter is read off the prime divisor
179	  `⟨P, h⟩` with `h : Order.coheight P = 1` the codimension-one witness
180	  automatic for a closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme).
181	
182	The iff is the substantive content of Hartshorne's Proposition 7.7(b) /
183	its proof, specialised to `D_0 = [P]`.
184	
185	iter-177+ body: unfold `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` as the subsheaf of
186	`𝒦_C` generated locally by `f_P⁻¹` near `P` and by `1` elsewhere, then
187	read off the order conditions at each stalk via the DVR valuation
188	identification.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff`
191	(Hartshorne II.7 Proposition 7.7(b), p. 157). -/
192	lemma globalSections_iff
193	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
194	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
195	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
196	    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
197	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
198	    (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
199	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
200	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f
201	    ↔
202	    Nonempty
203	      { s : Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0 //
204	        s ≠ 0 } := by
205	  sorry

207	/-! ## §3. Cohomological vanishing in genus zero
208	
209	Specialise Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's inductive step at `D = 0`. The
210	standard short exact sequence
211	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0` (Hartshorne II.6.18: the ideal sheaf
212	of the locally principal closed subscheme `P` is `𝒪_C(−[P])`; the
213	quotient is the skyscraper `k(P) ≅ k̄` at `P`) tensored by the locally
214	free rank-`1` sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` (left rigid, so preserves exactness and
215	leaves the skyscraper invariant) becomes
216	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0` in `Coh(C)`. The associated long exact
217	sequence of sheaf cohomology, combined with `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0`
218	(genus-`0` hypothesis: `g(C) = dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`) and
219	`H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper / flasque), kills `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))`. -/
220	
221	/-- **Vanishing of `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))` on a smooth proper geometrically
222	irreducible curve of genus `0`** (Hartshorne IV §1 p. 296, the
223	inductive step of Theorem 1.3 specialised to `D = 0`).
224	
225	Concretely, the finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector space
226	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 1` has dimension
227	`0`, i.e. is the trivial vector space.
228	
229	iter-177+ body: assemble the closed-point short exact sequence
230	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`, feed it to the long exact sequence of
231	`Module k̄`-flavoured cohomology (the project's
232	`Scheme.HModule k̄`-bridge inherits the LES by forget-functor
233	naturality from
234	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence_exact`), substitute
235	`H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (the genus-`0` hypothesis, unfolding
236	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`) and `H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper sheaf /
237	flasque cohomology, Hartshorne III.2.5), and collapse the segment to
238	`0 → H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) → 0`.
239	
240	Blueprint reference: `lem:H1_vanishing_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_genusZero`
241	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 296). -/
242	lemma h1_vanishing_genusZero
243	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
244	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
245	    Module.finrank kbar
246	        (Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 1) = 0 := by
247	  sorry

249	/-! ## §4. The dimension formula `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus zero
250	
251	Specialise the Euler-characteristic identity
252	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` of `RR.2`
253	(`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`) to
254	`D = [P]`. Since `deg([P]) = 1` (every closed point contributes degree
255	`1` over `k̄`) and `g(C) = 0`, this gives `χ(𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. Unfolding
256	`χ` as `dim H⁰ − dim H¹` and substituting the `H¹`-vanishing of §3
257	yields `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. -/
258	
259	/-- **The dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` on a smooth
260	proper geometrically irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄`**
261	(Hartshorne IV §1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297).
262	
263	iter-177+ body: invoke
264	`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` on the
265	`ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP` (matching the

267	`lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP` with
268	`WeilDivisor.sheafOf (ofClosedPoint P hP)`), evaluate the right-hand
269	side `deg([P]) + 1 − g(C) = 1 + 1 − 0 = 2`, unfold
270	`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic` as
271	`(Module.finrank kbar H⁰) − (Module.finrank kbar H¹)`, substitute
272	`Module.finrank kbar H¹ = 0` from `h1_vanishing_genusZero`, and read
273	off `Module.finrank kbar H⁰ = 2`.
274

276	(Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/

278	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
279	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :

281	        (Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0) = 2 := by
282	  sorry

284	/-! ## §5. A non-constant rational function with at most a simple pole at `P`
285	
286	The two-dimensionality of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` and the one-dimensional
287	constant subspace `k̄ · 1` give a non-zero quotient `H⁰/k̄`. Any lift
288	of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
289	`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
290	the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
291	This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
292	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/
293	
294	/-- **Existence of a non-constant rational function regular on `C ∖ {P}`
295	with at most a simple pole at `P`** (Hartshorne IV §1 Exercise 1.1,
296	p. 297, the genus-`0` specialisation).
297	
298	Concretely, there exists `f ∈ K(C)` such that:
299	
300	* `f ≠ 0`;
301	* `f ∉ k̄` (i.e. `f` is non-constant — for instance, it does not lie
302	  in the image of the structural inclusion of constants);
303	* `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose
304	  generic point is not `P`;
305	* `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
306	
307	iter-177+ body: use `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` to get
308	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. The image of `1 ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) ≅ k̄`
309	under the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)` spans a one-dimensional
310	subspace of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`; choose any element of the complement
311	(non-empty because `dim H⁰ = 2 > 1`), lift it through
312	`globalSections_iff` to a rational function `f`, and verify that the
313	three bullets hold. The lift is non-constant because it is not in the
314	constant subspace.
315	
316	The principal-divisor-non-zero formulation `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
317	f hf ≠ 0` follows from non-constancy plus the fact that constant
318	functions have principal divisor zero (the converse — `div(f) = 0`
319	⇒ `f` constant — uses the integrality of `C` and is the Stacks 02P0
320	"functions with no zeros and poles are constant" type statement).
321	
322	Blueprint reference:
323	`cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (alias
324	`cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero` consumed by `RR.4`)
325	(Hartshorne IV.1 Exercise 1.1, p. 297). -/
326	theorem exists_nonconstant_genusZero
327	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
328	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
329	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
330	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
331	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
332	    ∃ (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0),
333	      (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
334	          0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
335	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f ∧
336	      Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (X := C.left) f hf ≠ 0 := by
337	  sorry
338	
339	end lineBundleAtClosedPoint

13	This file is the **RR.2** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's

15	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in

17

19	through:

22	  divisor group `Div(C)` and the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- **`RR.2` (this file, `RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`)**: the Riemann–Roch
24	  dimension formula `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0` (with `deg D ≥ 0`),

31	- `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`, future): the "two-section
32	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.

100	/-! ## §1. The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve -/
101	
102	/-- **Euler characteristic of a `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf on a smooth proper
103	curve `C / k̄`.**
104	
105	On a curve (one-dimensional scheme), Grothendieck vanishing
106	(Hartshorne III.2.7) gives `H^i(C, 𝓕) = 0` for `i ≥ 2`, so the classical
107	alternating sum
108	`χ(𝓕) = Σ_{i ≥ 0} (-1)^i dim_{k̄} H^i(C, 𝓕)` collapses to the two-term
109	expression
110	`χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)`.
111	
112	This is the definition we ship. Coherence of `𝓕` on the proper `k̄`-scheme
113	`C` guarantees that both `H⁰` and `H¹` are finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector
114	spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same
115	finiteness backing `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`), so the two `Module.finrank`s
116	are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.
117	
118	The `Module k̄`-valued cohomology pipeline is the project's
119	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009), the same wrapper used by
120	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`.
121	
122	Blueprint reference: `def:eulerChar_curve`
123	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295, displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3). -/
124	noncomputable def Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
125	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
126	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
127	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
128	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
129	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
130	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : ℤ :=
131	  (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 0) : ℤ)
132	    - (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 1) : ℤ)
133	
134	/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (placeholder)
135	
136	The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
137	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of
138	closed points are glued into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. To keep the
139	iter-174 skeleton's pin signatures substantive, we expose `𝒪_C(D)` as a
140	typed-`sorry` `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf-on-`C`. The iter-175+ closure of
141	`RR.3` will replace the body with the construction; downstream pins (the
142	`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) consume
143	this placeholder only through its `H⁰` and `H¹`, so their signatures
144	encode arithmetic identities on finiteness-of-cohomology outputs and are
145	not vacuous. -/
146	
147	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
148	
149	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
150	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
151	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
152	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
153	
154	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper
155	curve `C / k̄`** (placeholder for `RR.3`).
156	
157	For a Weil divisor `D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` on a smooth proper geometrically
158	irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` is the
159	locally-free `𝒪_C`-module of rank one cut out (locally near each `Pᵢ`) by
160	the `(−nᵢ)`-th power of the local uniformiser at `Pᵢ`. Hartshorne II §6
161	Proposition 6.13 packages this construction; the project-side `RR.3`
162	chapter (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, to be added) will produce the carrier as
163	a `Sheaf` of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules on `C.left`.
164	
165	In the iter-174 skeleton this is a typed-`sorry`. The body in `RR.3` will
166	be the invertible-sheaf glue of the locally principal ideals of the
167	support of `D`. -/
168	noncomputable def sheafOf (_D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
169	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
170	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
171	  sorry
172	
173	/-! ## §3. The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor -/
174	
175	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
176	`C / k̄`.**
177	
178	By definition,
179	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
180	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
181	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
182	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
183	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
184	of `genus C`.
185	
186	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
187	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
188	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
191	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
192	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
193	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
194	
195	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
196	
197	/-! ## §4. The χ-identity: `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`
198	
199	The bridge from the structural Euler characteristic to the arithmetic
200	degree of a divisor. Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3 reduces Riemann–Roch to
201	this identity; the proof is the inductive `D ↔ D + [P]` step (additivity
202	of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence, with base case `D = 0`
203	giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` from `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` and the definition of
204	the genus). The closure of the body is iter-175+ work after the
205	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style
206	project-side helper is supplied and the `RR.3` sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` has a
207	body. -/
208	
209	/-- **Euler-characteristic identity for `𝒪_C(D)` on a smooth proper curve
210	of genus `g`.**
211	
212	For every Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)`,
213	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g(C)`.
214	
215	The proof is Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's reduction: induction on the
216	free-abelian-group structure of `Div(C)` on closed points, base case `D =
217	0` giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` (the `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` is the Hartshorne
218	I.3.4 input via the project's `H⁰`-bridge), and inductive step via
219	additivity of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence
220	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`.
221	
222	Blueprint reference: `thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus`
223	(Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3, p. 295). -/
224	theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus
225	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
226	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
227	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
228	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
229	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
230	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D)
231	      = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D) + 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) :=
232	  sorry
233	
234	/-! ## §5. The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero -/
235	
236	/-- **Riemann–Roch in genus zero (Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5).**
237	
238	Let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over the
239	algebraically closed field `k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, and let `D ∈ Div(C)` with
240	`deg D ≥ 0`. Then
241	`ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1`.
242	
243	The proof specialises the χ-identity
244	`eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` to `g = 0`,
245	unfolds `χ` via `def:eulerChar_curve`, and absorbs the `H¹`-vanishing
246	hypothesis (named premise `_hH1`: `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0`, which is the
247	`H¹`-vanishing of a non-negative-degree invertible sheaf on a
248	genus-`0` curve, to be discharged by `RR.3` once `𝒪_C(D)` has a body and
249	the cohomology of `𝒪_{ℙ¹}(d)` is computed).
250	
251	Blueprint reference: `thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero` (Hartshorne IV.1
252	Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
253	theorem Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero
254	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
255	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
256	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
257	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
258	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0)
259	    (_hdeg : (0 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D)
260	    (_hH1 : Module.finrank kbar
261	      (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) 1) = 0) :
262	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.l (C := C) D : ℤ)
263	      = Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D + 1 :=
264	  sorry
265	
266	end AlgebraicGeometry
267

9	/-!
10	# Weil divisors on a smooth proper curve (RR.1)
11	
12	This file is the **RR.1** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
13	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
14	isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
15	
16	Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `WeilDivisor` on a scheme; adjacent pieces
17	(`MeromorphicOn.divisor`, `CommRing.Pic`, `Scheme.RationalMap`) cover different
18	ground. This file is therefore **project-bespoke**, scaffolding the formal-sum data
19	type `Div(X) = ⨁_{Y ⊂ X codim 1} ℤ` on a Noetherian integral scheme `X` satisfying
20	Hartshorne's condition `(*)`, the principal-divisor homomorphism
21	`div : K(X)^× → Div(X)` on a curve, the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ` on a smooth
22	proper curve over an algebraically closed field, the degree-zero of principal divisors
23	on a complete nonsingular curve (Hartshorne Cor. II.6.10), and the linear-equivalence
24	relation `D ~ D'`.
25	
26	## Status (iter-172 file-skeleton)
27	
28	This file is the **iter-172 Lane C** file-skeleton: each declaration carries the
29	intended signature (matching the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin) with a `sorry` body.
30	The bodies are iter-173+ work after the sibling chapters `RR.2`
31	(`RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`), `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), and `RR.4`
32	(`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`) land.
33	
34	The 9 pinned declarations are:
35	
36	1. `Scheme.WeilDivisor` — free abelian group on prime divisors (Definition).
37	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.order` — order of a rational function along a prime divisor.
38	3. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint` — Weil divisor associated to a closed point.
39	4. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree` — degree map over an algebraically closed base.
40	5. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_hom` — degree is a group homomorphism (Theorem).
41	6. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal` — principal divisor of a rational function.
42	7. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_hom` — `div` is a group homomorphism (Theorem).

54	set_option autoImplicit false
