56	universe u
57	
58	open CategoryTheory Limits
59	
60	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
61	
62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
101	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
102	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
105	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
106	
107	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
108	
109	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
110	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
111	
112	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
113	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
114	
115	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
116	
117	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
118	
119	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
120	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
121	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
122	
123	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
124	
125	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
126	
127	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
128	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
129	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
130	discrete valuation on `f`.
131	
132	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
133	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
136	
137	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
138	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
139	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
140	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
141	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
142	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
143	
144	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
145	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
146	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
147	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
149	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
150	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
151	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
152	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
153	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
154	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
155	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
156	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
157	
158	end Scheme.RationalMap
159	
160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
170	
171	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a Krull-dim-≤-1 stalk. -/
173	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) : Prop where
174	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk has Krull dimension at
175	  most one. (For a regular-in-codim-1 scheme the stalk is in fact a DVR; we
176	  only export the weaker Krull-dim bound here, sufficient to invoke
177	  `Ring.ordFrac`.) -/
178	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
179	
180	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
181	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
182	prime divisor `Y`. -/
183	instance {X : Scheme.{u}} [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
184	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
185	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
186	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
187	
188	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
189	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
190	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
191	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
192	
193	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
194	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
195	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
196	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
197	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
198	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
199	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
200	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
201	
202	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
203	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
204	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
205	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
206	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
207	    (f : X.functionField) :
208	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
209	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
210	  sorry
211	
212	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
213	
214	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
215	
216	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
217	
218	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
219	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
220	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
221	
222	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
223	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
224	
225	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
226	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
227	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
228	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
229	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
230	
231	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
232	
233	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
234	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
235	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
236	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
237	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
238	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
239	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
240	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
241	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
242	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
243	hypothesised regime. -/
244	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
245	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
246	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
247	
248	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
249	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
250	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
251	multiplicity one. -/
252	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
253	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
254	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
255	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
256	
257	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
258	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
259	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
260	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
261	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
262	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
263	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
264	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
265	
266	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
267	
268	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
269	
270	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
271	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
272	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
273	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
274	
275	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
276	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
277	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
278	
279	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
280	
281	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
282	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
283	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
284	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
285	
286	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
287	
288	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
289	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
290	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
291	
292	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
293	
294	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
295	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
296	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
297	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
298	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
299	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
300	
301	@[simp]
302	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
303	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
304	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
305	
306	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
307	
308	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
309	
310	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
311	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
312	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
313	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
314	
315	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
316	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
319	following definition, p. 131).
320	
321	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
322	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
323	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
324	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
325	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
326	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
327	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
328	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
329	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
330	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
331	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
332	
333	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
334	
335	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
336	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
337	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
340	
341	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
342	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
343	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
344	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
345	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
346	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
347	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
348	  toFun u :=
349	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
350	  map_one' := by
351	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
352	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
353	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
354	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
355	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
356	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
357	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
358	    congr 1
359	    apply Finsupp.ext
360	    intro Y
361	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
362	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
363	    rw [Units.val_one]
364	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
365	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
366	  map_mul' u v := by
367	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
368	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
369	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
370	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
371	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
372	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
373	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
374	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
375	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
376	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
377	    congr 1
378	    apply Finsupp.ext
379	    intro Y
380	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
381	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
382	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
383	    rw [Units.val_mul]
384	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
385	    rw [map_mul]
386	    exact WithZero.log_mul
387	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
388	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
389	
390	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
391	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
392	
393	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
394	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
395	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
396	
397	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
398	
399	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
400	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
401	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
402	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
403	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
404	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
405	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
406	iters of `RR.1`. -/
407	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
408	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
409	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
410	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
411	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
412	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
413	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 := by
414	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10 (iter-178 partial). Case-split on whether every
415	  -- prime-divisor order of `f` vanishes. On a complete nonsingular curve,
416	  -- this case split exactly recovers Hartshorne's constant-vs-non-constant
417	  -- split: `(∀ Y, ord_Y f = 0)` ⟺ `f ∈ k̄ \ {0}` (a nowhere-vanishing
418	  -- rational function on a complete curve is constant; that direction is
419	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10's "if `f ∈ k̄` then `(f) = 0`").
420	  by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
421	  · -- Constant branch: every order vanishes ⟹ `principal f hf = 0` ⟹
422	    -- `degree (principal f hf) = degree 0 = 0`.
423	    have hprincipal_zero : principal f hf = 0 := by
424	      apply Finsupp.ext
425	      intro Y
426	      change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
427	      exact hconst Y
428	    rw [hprincipal_zero]
429	    unfold degree
430	    exact Finsupp.sum_zero_index
431	  · -- Non-constant branch (Hartshorne II.6.10 `f ∉ k̄`): the inclusion
432	    -- `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` defines a finite morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the
433	    -- function-field-determines-curve correspondence (Hartshorne I.6.12),
434	    -- and `principal f hf = φ^*([0] - [∞])`. Pullback along a finite
435	    -- morphism of curves multiplies degrees by `deg(φ)` (Hartshorne
436	    -- II.6.9), so `deg(principal f hf) = deg(φ) · deg([0] - [∞]) =
437	    -- deg(φ) · 0 = 0`.
438	    -- This branch is gated on (i) the `φ : C → ℙ¹` construction (Lane 5
439	    -- `RationalCurveIso.lean:morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`, iter-178+),
440	    -- and (ii) the degree-multiplicativity-under-finite-pullback bridge
441	    -- (Hartshorne II.6.9, Mathlib gap).
442	    sorry
443	
444	/-! ## §6. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
445	
446	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
447	
448	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
449	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
450	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
451	
452	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
453	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
454	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
455	**divisor class group** of `X`.
456	
457	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
458	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
459	
460	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
461	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
462	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
463	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
464	
465	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
466	
467	end AlgebraicGeometry
468

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form)
10	
11	A self-contained Phase E helper: two morphisms `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between
12	schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree
13	as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and geometrically irreducible over
14	`Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` — agree everywhere. Independent of
15	`AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian C` and provable from current Mathlib alone
16	(Stacks 0BFD plus standard separatedness/irreducibility lemmas).
17	
18	The historical motivation is Mumford's §4 rigidity for morphisms of group
19	schemes; the formalized statement is the minimal-hypothesis scheme-level
20	form, which the Mumford group-scheme rigidity follows from by specialization.
21	
22	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Rigidity.tex`.
23	
24	## Status
25	
26	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` — rigidity of morphisms agreeing as scheme
27	maps on a non-empty open of a reduced (geometrically) irreducible source
28	with separated target — is closed. See the "Hypothesis history" block below
29	for the discussion of why the original point-wise hypothesis was
30	strengthened to scheme-level equality on `U`.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
42	
43	/-!
44	## Hypothesis history
45	
46	### Scheme-level hypothesis (iter 003)
47	
48	The original scaffold from iter 002 had hypothesis
49	`h : ∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x`
50	(point-wise topological equality on `U`). This is too weak: in characteristic
51	`p`, the absolute Frobenius `F : X → X` of an elliptic curve over `𝔽_p` agrees
52	topologically with the identity on all of `X` (the identity on the underlying
53	topological space) but `F ≠ id` as scheme morphisms — they differ on stalks
54	(`a ↦ a` vs `a ↦ a^p`). Hence the symmetric-form rigidity statement
55	`g₁.base = g₂.base on U ⇒ g₁ = g₂` is mathematically false.
56	
57	We strengthen the hypothesis to scheme-level equality on `U`:
58	`h : U.ι ≫ g₁.left = U.ι ≫ g₂.left`. This is the version that is actually
59	needed at the use site (uniqueness half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`):
60	the application produces *scheme-level* agreement on a non-empty open
61	(through the symmetric-power surjection), not merely topological agreement.
62	
63	We also add `[IsReduced X.left]` because Mathlib `b80f227` lacks the lemma
64	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` for schemes over a field (the closest is the perfect-base
65	case `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`). This is a small Mathlib
66	gap that the downstream consumer must satisfy via the smoothness + irreducibility
67	of the curve / Jacobian (e.g. via `IsIntegral`).
68	
69	### Unused-hypothesis cleanup (iter 125)
70	
71	Eight decorative hypotheses (`{n m : ℕ}`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n X.hom]`,
72	`[IsProper X.hom]`, `[GrpObj X]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension m Y.hom]`,
73	`[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]`, `[GrpObj Y]`, and the strengthening of
74	`[IsSeparated Y.hom]` to `[IsProper Y.hom]`) were dropped per
75	`analogies/rigidity-refactor.md`. The declaration was also renamed from
76	`GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen` to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`, mirroring
77	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` naming idiom
78	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated:319–322`).
79	-/
80	
81	/-- Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form): two morphisms
82	`g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a
83	non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and
84	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` —
85	agree everywhere.
86	
87	This is the `Over (Spec (.of k))`-bundled specialization of Mathlib's
88	`ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The Mumford group-scheme rigidity
89	statement (a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is zero) is
90	interderivable with this form using the group law on the target. -/
91	theorem Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen
92	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    [IsSeparated Y.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
95	    [IsReduced X.left]
96	    (g₁ g₂ : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set X.left).Nonempty)
97	    (h : (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₁.left =
98	      (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₂.left) :
99	    g₁ = g₂ := by
100	  -- (1) Provide the separatedness instance under the `OverClass.fromOver`
101	  --     form `Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k)` so that the downstream typeclass
102	  --     search at `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` finds it.
103	  haveI : IsSeparated (Y.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
104	    (‹IsSeparated Y.hom› : IsSeparated Y.hom)
105	  -- (2) `X.left` is irreducible: `Spec k` is a single point (`Unique`,
106	  --     hence `Subsingleton + Nonempty`), and `GeometricallyIrreducible`
107	  --     over a subsingleton base implies `IrreducibleSpace`.
108	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X.left :=
109	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton X.hom
110	  -- (3) `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X.left` is dominant: a non-empty open in an
111	  --     irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant.
112	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) :=
113	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
114	  -- (4) Promote `g₁ = g₂` from the underlying scheme equality.
115	  --     The `Scheme.Over` and `IsOver` instances for `X.left`, `Y.left`,
116	  --     `g₁.left`, `g₂.left` over `Spec (.of k)` are inferred globally
117	  --     from the `Over (Spec (.of k))` category structure
118	  --     (`OverClass.fromOver`).
119	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
120	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of k))
121	    (X := X.left) (Y := Y.left) (f := g₁.left) (g := g₂.left) U.ι h
122	
123	end AlgebraicGeometry
124

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity over `k̄`: morphisms `ℙ¹_{k̄} → A_{k̄}` are constant
10	
11	The keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-0
12	Albanese-witness argument (Jacobian.tex § C.2): morphisms from the
13	projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
14	group scheme `A_{k̄}` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` that hit
15	the identity at a `k̄`-rational point are the constant morphism at the
16	identity. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Chapter~II~§4.
17	
18	See `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`.
19	
20	## Status (iter-126 scaffold)
21	
22	The named declaration `rigidity_over_kbar` is scaffolded with a single
23	`sorry` body. The body closure is gated on the shared cotangent-
24	vanishing Mathlib pile (`analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md`,
25	iter-129+) per STRATEGY.md § M2.a + § M2.d-alt. The proof decomposition
26	into sub-steps C.2.b (reduction to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`),
27	C.2.c (image-dimension dichotomy), C.2.d (the cotangent-vanishing
28	keystone), and C.2.e (set-to-scheme promotion) is documented in
29	`RigidityKbar.tex` § "Proof decomposition".
30	
31	## Encoding choice (iter-126 refactor agent note)
32	
33	The directive offered two encodings of the source `ℙ¹_{k̄}`: a literal
34	`Over.mk (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom MvPolynomial.C))` (Option A) or
35	an abstract "smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄` of
36	genus 0" (Option B). The literal Option A is mathematically wrong as
37	written — `Spec(MvPolynomial σ kbar) ⟶ Spec kbar` is the affine, not
38	projective, line — and Mathlib `b80f227` has no `ProjectiveSpace n S`
39	construction packaged as a `Scheme` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj` is the
40	general `Proj` of a graded ring, not specialised). The refactor agent
41	took the directive-recommended Option B: the source is a smooth proper
42	geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of relative dimension
43	`1` with `genus C = 0`. This is what the proof decomposition C.2.c–C.2.d
44	actually consume (the keystone uses the curve's `H¹(·, 𝒪) = 0` ↔
45	`genus = 0` characterisation, not a literal `ℙ¹` API).
46	-/
47	
48	set_option autoImplicit false
49	
50	universe u
51	
52	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
53	
54	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
55	
56	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
57	
58	/-- Rigidity for morphisms `C ⟶ A` from a genus-`0` curve over an
59	algebraically closed field `k̄` (encoding `ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the abstract
60	smooth-proper-geometrically-irreducible-of-relative-dimension-`1`-with-
61	`genus = 0` characterisation): every morphism from such a curve to a
62	smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `A` over `k̄`
63	that hits the identity at a `k̄`-rational point is the constant
64	morphism at the identity.
65	
66	This is the keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the
67	genus-0 Albanese-witness argument. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*,
68	Chapter~II~§4.
69	
70	**Status**: iter-126 scaffold — body is a single `sorry`. The closure
71	(C.2.b reduction via `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` + C.2.c image-
72	dimension dichotomy + C.2.d cotangent-vanishing keystone) is gated on
73	the shared cotangent-vanishing Mathlib pile (iter-129+). See
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`. -/
75	theorem rigidity_over_kbar
76	    [IsAlgClosed kbar] [CharZero kbar]
77	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
78	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
79	    [IsProper C.hom]
80	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
81	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
82	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
83	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
84	    (f : C ⟶ A)
85	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
86	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
87	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) :=
88	  sorry
89	
90	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Mumford Rigidity Lemma and its Milne §I.1 corollaries
10	
11	This file is the **abstract rigidity foundation** of the project's genus-`0` route. It hosts the
12	proven, axiom-clean **Mumford Rigidity Lemma (Form I)** and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries it
13	implies — the additive decomposition of a morphism out of a product (Cor 1.5) and the fact that
14	a pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a homomorphism (Cor 1.2).
15	
16	The genus-`0` final assembly (the `ℙ¹`-scaling shortcut, the headline
17	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`, the Riemann–Roch bridge `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`) lives
18	downstream in `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, which imports this file.
19	
20	## Contents
21	
22	The Rigidity-Lemma chain (PROVEN axiom-clean, iters 157–162; cube-free, cohomology-free, char-free):
23	
24	* `rigidity_snd_lift` — the cartesian-monoidal skeleton step of the Rigidity Lemma.
25	* `snd_left_isClosedMap` — bridge 1: completeness of `X` makes `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` a closed map.
26	* `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints` — the dense-closed-points hom-extensionality connective
27	  (route B's Step 2).
28	* `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral` — the deep algebra of bridge 2 / Step 1: a proper
29	  integral `k̄`-scheme mapping to an affine is constant on `k̄`-points.
30	* `isIntegral_of_retract` — integrality descends to a retract; closes the slice/section
31	  assembly of route B's Step 1.
32	* `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine` — route B Step 1 assembled.
33	* `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine` — bridge 2 (slice-constancy) end to end.
34	* `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` — the genuine geometric content: a non-empty open of agreement.
35	* `rigidity_core` — the geometric core `f = retract ≫ f`.
36	* `rigidity_lemma` — **Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I).** The headline.
37	
38	The Milne §I.1 corollaries (both PROVEN axiom-clean from `rigidity_lemma`):
39	
40	* `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` — Corollary 1.5 (additive decomposition over a product).
41	* `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` — Corollary 1.2 (a pointed regular map of abelian varieties
42	  is a homomorphism).
43	
44	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
45	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §4; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Cor 1.2, Cor 1.5).
46	-/

56	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]

58	/-- **Cartesian-monoidal identity (skeleton step of the Rigidity Lemma).** Post-composing the
59	second projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` with the slice section `y ↦ (x₀, y)` is the "collapse the
60	`X`-axis onto `x₀`" endomorphism `(x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)` of `X ⊗ Y`:
61	`snd ≫ lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) = lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`.
62	
63	Pure cartesian-monoidal algebra (no geometry): `comp_lift` distributes the `snd`, the
64	`𝟙 Y` component simplifies by `Category.comp_id`, and the `toUnit Y` component collapses by
65	uniqueness of maps into the terminal object. -/
66	theorem rigidity_snd_lift
67	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
68	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X) :
69	    snd X Y ≫ lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) =
70	      lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) := by
71	  ext1 <;> simp
72	
73	/-- **Bridge 1 of the Rigidity Lemma (closed-map step), PROVEN.** When `X` is complete (proper)
74	over `k̄`, the second monoidal projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` has, on underlying schemes, a
75	*closed* base map. This is Mumford's "completeness of `X` makes `p₂` a closed map" (Abelian
76	Varieties, Ch. II §4, p. 43).
77	
78	Proof: the underlying scheme morphism `(snd X Y).left` is the pullback projection
79	`Limits.pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom` (`Over.snd_left`), i.e. the base change of `X.hom` along
80	`Y.hom`. `IsProper X.hom ⟹ UniversallyClosed X.hom` (`IsProper.toUniversallyClosed`), and
81	`UniversallyClosed` is stable under base change
82	(`universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback` on the canonical pullback square), so
83	`(snd X Y).left` is universally closed and hence its base map is closed
84	(`Scheme.Hom.isClosedMap`). Char-free; no theorem of the cube, no cohomology. -/
85	theorem snd_left_isClosedMap
86	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper X.hom] :
87	    IsClosedMap (snd X Y).left.base := by
88	  haveI hp : UniversallyClosed X.hom := IsProper.toUniversallyClosed
89	  haveI : UniversallyClosed (snd X Y).left := by
90	    rw [Over.snd_left]
91	    exact universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback
92	      (IsPullback.of_hasPullback X.hom Y.hom) hp
93	  exact Scheme.Hom.isClosedMap _
94	
95	/-- **Dense-closed-points hom-extensionality (the bespoke globalisation connective, PROVEN).**
96	Two morphisms `g₁ g₂ : W ⟶ Z` out of a *reduced* scheme `W` whose closed points are *dense*
97	(`[JacobsonSpace W]` — e.g. when `W` is locally of finite type over a field) into a *separated*
98	scheme `Z` are equal as soon as they agree at every closed point `x ∈ closedPoints W` after the
99	canonical residue-field probe `W.fromSpecResidueField x : Spec κ(x) ⟶ W`.
100	
101	This is the one connective the iter-159 `mathlib-analogist` flagged that Mathlib does **not**
102	package directly: Mathlib supplies only the single-dominant-morphism `ext_of_isDominant`. Here we
103	assemble all the closed points into one dominant probe — the coproduct
104	`∐_{x ∈ closedPoints W} Spec κ(x) ⟶ W`, whose topological range is exactly the (dense) set of
105	closed points — and feed it to `ext_of_isDominant`. It is `Step 2` of bridge 2's route B
106	(cohomology-free) and is fully proven here, reusable independently of the rigidity context. -/
107	theorem morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints
108	    {W Z : Scheme.{u}} [IsReduced W] [JacobsonSpace W] [Z.IsSeparated]
109	    {g₁ g₂ : W ⟶ Z}
110	    (h : ∀ x ∈ closedPoints W,
111	      W.fromSpecResidueField x ≫ g₁ = W.fromSpecResidueField x ≫ g₂) :
112	    g₁ = g₂ := by
113	  -- The dominant probe: the coproduct of the residue-field `Spec`s over the closed points.
114	  let F : closedPoints W → Scheme.{u} := fun x => Spec (W.residueField x.1)
115	  let probe : (∐ F) ⟶ W := Sigma.desc fun x => W.fromSpecResidueField x.1
116	  -- Its topological range contains every closed point, hence (Jacobson) is dense.
117	  haveI : IsDominant probe := by
118	    refine ⟨(dense_iff_closure_eq.mpr (closure_closedPoints (X := W))).mono ?_⟩
119	    intro x hx
120	    obtain ⟨pt⟩ : Nonempty (Spec (W.residueField x)) := inferInstance
121	    refine ⟨(Sigma.ι F ⟨x, hx⟩).base pt, ?_⟩
122	    have hcomp : Sigma.ι F ⟨x, hx⟩ ≫ probe = W.fromSpecResidueField x := Sigma.ι_desc _ _
123	    have e1 : probe.base ((Sigma.ι F ⟨x, hx⟩).base pt) = (W.fromSpecResidueField x).base pt := by
124	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, hcomp]
125	    rw [e1]
126	    exact Set.eq_of_mem_singleton (Scheme.range_fromSpecResidueField x ▸ Set.mem_range_self pt)
127	  -- Componentwise the probe equalises `g₁` and `g₂`; dominance then forces `g₁ = g₂`.
128	  refine ext_of_isDominant probe (Sigma.hom_ext _ _ fun x => ?_)
129	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc]
130	  exact h x.1 x.2
131	
132	/-- **A proper integral `k̄`-scheme mapping into an affine is constant on `k̄`-points (the deep
133	algebraic content of Step 1, PROVEN).** Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, let `W` be an
134	integral scheme that is universally closed and locally of finite type over `Spec k̄` (e.g. a proper
135	integral slice `X_y`), and let `g : W ⟶ V` be a morphism into an *affine* scheme `V`. Then `g`
136	takes the same value on any two `k̄`-points (sections `a`, `b` of the structure map `wk`):
137	`a ≫ wk = 𝟙` and `b ≫ wk = 𝟙` force `a ≫ g = b ≫ g`.
138	
139	This is the cohomology-free realisation of "a global regular function on a proper integral
140	`k̄`-variety is constant". The global sections `Γ(W, ⊤)` form a field
141	(`isField_of_universallyClosed`) that is module-finite over `k̄`
142	(`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`, hence the structure map is integral on `Γ`); algebraic
143	closedness collapses the finite extension (`IsAlgClosed.ringHom_bijective_of_isIntegral`), so the
144	structure map's global-sections map `wk.appTop` is an isomorphism. Both sections `a`, `b` are
145	left inverses of `wk.appTop` on `Γ`, hence have equal `appTop`; and a morphism into the affine `V`
146	is pinned by its `appTop` (`ext_of_isAffine`). No coherent cohomology, no relative Stein
147	factorisation. -/
148	theorem eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral
149	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
150	    {W : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral W] (wk : W ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))
151	    [UniversallyClosed wk] [LocallyOfFiniteType wk]
152	    {V : Scheme.{u}} [IsAffine V] (g : W ⟶ V)
153	    (a b : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶ W)
154	    (ha : a ≫ wk = 𝟙 _) (hb : b ≫ wk = 𝟙 _) :
155	    a ≫ g = b ≫ g := by
156	  -- `Γ(W)` is a field (proper integral over `k̄`).
157	  letI : Field Γ(W, ⊤) := (isField_of_universallyClosed (CommRingCat.of kbar) wk).toField
158	  -- `F : k̄ ⟶ Γ(W)` (the structure ring map up to `ΓSpecIso`) is integral, hence — `k̄` alg-closed,
159	  -- `Γ(W)` a domain — bijective, so an iso.
160	  set F : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ Γ(W, ⊤) :=
161	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ wk.appTop with hF
162	  have hint : F.hom.IsIntegral := by
163	    apply RingHom.isIntegral_respectsIso.2 (e := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv)
164	    exact isIntegral_appTop_of_universallyClosed wk
165	  haveI : IsIso F := (ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective F).mpr
166	    (IsAlgClosed.ringHom_bijective_of_isIntegral F.hom hint)
167	  -- Hence `wk.appTop = ΓSpecIso.hom ≫ F` is an iso.
168	  haveI : IsIso wk.appTop := by
169	    have heq : wk.appTop = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).hom ≫ F := by
170	      rw [hF]; simp
171	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
172	  -- Both sections invert `wk.appTop` on global sections, so they have equal `appTop`.
173	  have haa : wk.appTop ≫ a.appTop = 𝟙 _ := by rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ha]; simp
174	  have hbb : wk.appTop ≫ b.appTop = 𝟙 _ := by rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, hb]; simp
175	  have hab : a.appTop = b.appTop := by rw [← cancel_epi wk.appTop, haa, hbb]
176	  -- A map into the affine `V` is pinned by `appTop`.
177	  apply ext_of_isAffine
178	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, hab]
179	
180	/-- **Integrality descends to a retract (blueprint `lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral`).**
181	If `T` is an integral scheme and `S` is a *retract* of `T` — i.e. there are `r : S ⟶ T` and
182	`pr : T ⟶ S` with `r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S` — then `S` is integral.
183	
184	Two halves, both elementary (no cohomology):
185	* *Irreducible.* `pr` is a continuous surjection (it has the section `r`, so `pr.base ∘ r.base =
186	  id`), and the continuous surjective image of the irreducible `T` is irreducible.
187	* *Reduced.* For each `x : S`, the stalk map `pr.stalkMap (r x) : 𝒪_{S,x} ⟶ 𝒪_{T, r x}` is split
188	  injective: composing with `r.stalkMap x` gives `(r ≫ pr).stalkMap x`, an isomorphism (since
189	  `r ≫ pr = 𝟙`). Hence `𝒪_{S,x}` embeds into the reduced stalk `𝒪_{T, r x}`
190	  (`isReduced_of_injective`), so every stalk of `S` is reduced (`isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`).
191	
192	Reduced and irreducible together give `IsIntegral S`
193	(`isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`). This feeds the Step-1 geometric assembly, where
194	the proper slice `X_y ≅ X` must be presented as proper *integral*. -/
195	theorem isIntegral_of_retract {S T : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral T]
196	    (r : S ⟶ T) (pr : T ⟶ S) (hrp : r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S) : IsIntegral S := by
197	  -- `pr.base` is surjective: `r.base` is a section of it.
198	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective pr.base := by
199	    intro x
200	    refine ⟨r.base x, ?_⟩
201	    have h := congrArg (fun m => m.base x) hrp
202	    simpa using h
203	  -- Irreducibility: continuous surjective image of the irreducible `T`.
204	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace S := by
205	    rw [irreducibleSpace_def]
206	    have h := (IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ T).image pr.base
207	      pr.base.hom.continuous.continuousOn
208	    rwa [Set.image_univ, hsurj.range_eq] at h
209	  -- Reducedness: each stalk of `S` embeds into the corresponding reduced stalk of `T`.
210	  haveI hstalk : ∀ x : S, _root_.IsReduced (S.presheaf.stalk x) := by
211	    intro x
212	    -- `(r ≫ pr).stalkMap x` is an isomorphism (`r ≫ pr = 𝟙`), equal to
213	    -- `pr.stalkMap (r x) ≫ r.stalkMap x`, so the first factor is injective.
214	    haveI hiso : IsIso ((r ≫ pr).stalkMap x) := by
215	      rw [Scheme.Hom.stalkMap_congr_hom (r ≫ pr) (𝟙 S) hrp x, Scheme.Hom.stalkMap_id]
216	      exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((S.presheaf.stalkCongr _).hom ≫ 𝟙 _))
217	    rw [Scheme.Hom.stalkMap_comp] at hiso
218	    have hbij := (ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective
219	      (pr.stalkMap (r.base x) ≫ r.stalkMap x)).1 hiso
220	    have hinj : Function.Injective (pr.stalkMap (r.base x)).hom := by
221	      intro a b hab
222	      apply hbij.injective
223	      rw [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
224	      exact congrArg (r.stalkMap x) hab
225	    have hb : pr.base (r.base x) = x := by
226	      have h := congrArg (fun m => m.base x) hrp
227	      simpa using h
228	    have hred : _root_.IsReduced (S.presheaf.stalk (pr.base (r.base x))) :=
229	      isReduced_of_injective (pr.stalkMap (r.base x)).hom hinj
230	    rwa [hb] at hred
231	  haveI : IsReduced S := isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk S
232	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced S
233	
234	/-- **Per-closed-slice constancy (Step 1 of bridge 2's route B), the residual deep geometry.**
235	With the data of `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, fix a *closed* point `x` of the
236	`p₂`-saturated open `U ⊆ X ⊗ Y` on which `f` lands in the affine `U₀`. Then `f` and the collapsed
237	map `retract ≫ f` (`retract := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`, i.e. `(x,y) ↦ (x₀,y)`)
238	agree at `x` after the residue-field probe `U.fromSpecResidueField x`.
239	
240	Mumford's "for each `y ∈ V`, the complete slice `X × {y}` maps into the affine, hence to a single
241	point" step, realised cohomology-FREE. The intended proof (analogist route B, `analogies/
242	rigidity-affineconst.md`): the closed point `x` lies over a closed point `y = p₂(x) ∈ Vset` with
243	`κ(y) = k̄` (`[IsAlgClosed kbar]`, finite type); saturation `_hUV` puts the whole proper integral
244	slice `X_y ≅ X` inside `U`, so `f` maps `X_y` into the affine `U₀`. By
245	`isField_of_universallyClosed` + `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` + alg-closedness,
246	`Γ(X_y) = k̄`, so `f|X_y` factors through a single `k̄`-point of `U₀` (`ext_of_isAffine`) —
247	necessarily `f(x₀, y)`, which is exactly `(retract ≫ f)(x)`. The relative Stein / `f_*𝒪 = 𝒪`
248	framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap and is deliberately avoided.
249	
250	**Status (iter-162): PROVEN axiom-clean.** It is the per-point input that
251	`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints` globalises over the dense closed points. Extracted as a named
252	top-level obligation per the route-B decomposition; the geometric slice/section assembly is closed
253	via the `IsIntegral X.left` retract argument (`isIntegral_of_retract`). -/
254	theorem rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine
255	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
256	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
257	    [IsProper X.hom]
258	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
259	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
260	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
261	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
262	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
263	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
264	    (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens)
265	    (Vset : Set Y.left)
266	    (_hUV : (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) = (snd X Y).left.base ⁻¹' Vset)
267	    (U₀ : Z.left.Opens) (_hU₀ : IsAffineOpen U₀)
268	    (_hfU : ∀ u ∈ (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left), f.left.base u ∈ U₀)
269	    (x : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme)
270	    (_hx : x ∈ closedPoints (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme) :
271	    (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme.fromSpecResidueField x ≫
272	        ((U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫ f.left) =
273	      (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme.fromSpecResidueField x ≫
274	        ((U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫
275	          (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left) := by
276	  -- `x` is a closed point of the locally-of-finite-type `k̄`-scheme `U`, so (alg-closedness) it is
277	  -- a `k̄`-rational point: `pointOfClosedPoint` packages the residue-field probe as a `k̄`-point
278	  -- `px : Spec k̄ ⟶ U` with `Spec.map (residueFieldIsoBase …).hom ≫ U.fromSpecResidueField x = px`.
279	  have hxc : IsClosed {x} := _hx
280	  set wU : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) :=
281	    U.ι ≫ (X ⊗ Y).hom with hwU
282	  set px : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶ (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme :=
283	    pointOfClosedPoint wU x hxc with hpx
284	  -- Reduce the residue-field-probe goal to the `k̄`-point statement by cancelling the iso
285	  -- `e := Spec.map (residueFieldIsoBase …).hom` on the left (`e ≫ U.fromSpecResidueField x = px`).
286	  rw [← cancel_epi (Spec.map (residueFieldIsoBase wU x hxc).hom)]
287	  suffices h : px ≫ U.ι ≫ Over.Hom.left f =
288	      px ≫ U.ι ≫ Over.Hom.left (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f) by
289	    rw [hpx] at h
290	    simpa only [pointOfClosedPoint, Category.assoc] using h
291	  -- `q := px ≫ U.ι : Spec k̄ ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left` is the `k̄`-rational point at the closed point `x`;
292	  -- it is a *section* of the structure map `(X ⊗ Y).hom` (a genuine `k̄`-point of `X ⊗ Y`).
293	  set q : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left := px ≫ U.ι with hq
294	  have hqsec : q ≫ (X ⊗ Y).hom = 𝟙 _ := by
295	    rw [hq, Category.assoc]; exact pointOfClosedPoint_comp wU x hxc
296	  -- Rewrite the collapsed side `(retract ≫ f).left = retract.left ≫ f.left`.
297	  rw [Over.comp_left]
298	  -- It remains to prove the `k̄`-point slice-constancy
299	  --   `q ≫ f.left = q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left`,
300	  -- i.e. `f` agrees at the `k̄`-point `q = (x_X, y)` and its `X`-collapse `retract(q) = (x₀, y)`.
301	  -- Both points lie on the proper integral slice `X_y` over the `k̄`-point `y := q ≫ p₂`, which
302	  -- (saturation `_hUV`) lies entirely inside `U`, hence (`_hfU`) maps under `f` into the affine
303	  -- `U₀`. The deep content "a proper integral `k̄`-scheme into an affine is constant on
304	  -- `k̄`-points" is now-proven as `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral`: realising the slice
305	  -- as `X` via the section `s := lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ ŷ)` over the `k̄`-point `ŷ : 𝟙_ ⟶ Y`
306	  -- lifting `y`, one
307	  -- corestricts `(s ≫ f).left : X.left → Z.left` to `U₀.toScheme` and applies the sub-lemma to the
308	  -- two `k̄`-points (the `X`-coordinate of `q`, and its `X`-collapse) of the proper integral `X`.
309	  -- Name the collapse endomorphism `retract := (x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)`.
310	  set retract : X ⊗ Y ⟶ X ⊗ Y := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) with hretract
311	  -- `(toUnit X).left = X.hom`.
312	  have htoUnit : (toUnit X).left = X.hom := by simp
313	  -- Lift `q` to a genuine `k̄`-point `q̂ : 𝟙_ ⟶ X ⊗ Y` of the product (a section of `(X⊗Y).hom`),
314	  -- working in `Over (Spec k̄)` where the cartesian-monoidal algebra is clean.
315	  set qhat : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ X ⊗ Y := Over.homMk q hqsec with hqhat
316	  have hqhatL : qhat.left = q := rfl
317	  -- Its `X`- and `Y`-coordinates, and the slice section `sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y`, `x ↦ (x, ŷ)`.
318	  set yhat : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ Y := qhat ≫ snd X Y with hyhat
319	  set xq : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ X := qhat ≫ fst X Y with hxq
320	  set sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y := lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ yhat) with hsecdef
321	  clear_value qhat xq yhat
322	  -- Slice identities in `Over (Spec k̄)`: `q̂ = xq ≫ sec` and `q̂ ≫ retract = x₀ ≫ sec`.
323	  have hIover : qhat = xq ≫ sec := by
324	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
325	    · rw [Category.assoc, hsecdef, lift_fst, Category.comp_id]; exact hxq.symm
326	    · rw [Category.assoc, hsecdef, lift_snd, ← Category.assoc,
327	        toUnit_unique (xq ≫ toUnit X) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]; exact hyhat.symm
328	  have hIIover : qhat ≫ retract = x₀ ≫ sec := by
329	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
330	    · rw [hretract, hsecdef, Category.assoc, lift_fst, ← Category.assoc,
331	        toUnit_unique (qhat ≫ toUnit (X ⊗ Y)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_fst,
332	        Category.comp_id]
333	    · rw [hretract, hsecdef, Category.assoc, lift_snd, Category.assoc, lift_snd, ← Category.assoc,
334	        toUnit_unique (x₀ ≫ toUnit X) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp, hyhat]
335	  -- `sec.left` is a section of the first projection, exhibiting `X.left` as a retract.
336	  have hsecLfst : sec.left ≫ (fst X Y).left = 𝟙 X.left := by
337	    rw [← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_fst, Over.id_left]
338	  have hyhatL : yhat.left = q ≫ (snd X Y).left := by
339	    rw [hyhat, Over.comp_left]; exact congrArg (· ≫ Over.Hom.left (snd X Y)) hqhatL
340	  have hsecLsnd : sec.left ≫ (snd X Y).left = X.hom ≫ q ≫ (snd X Y).left := by
341	    rw [← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_snd, ← hyhatL]; simp [htoUnit]
342	  -- `IsIntegral X.left`: `X.left` is a retract of the integral product via the section `sec.left`.
343	  haveI : IsIntegral (X ⊗ Y).left := by
344	    haveI : IrreducibleSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
345	      GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ⊗ Y).hom
346	    exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _
347	  haveI : IsIntegral X.left := isIntegral_of_retract sec.left (fst X Y).left hsecLfst
348	  -- `sec ≫ f` maps the slice into the affine `U₀`; corestrict to `U₀.toScheme`.
349	  haveI : IsAffine U₀.toScheme := _hU₀
350	  -- The slice `sec.left` lands in the saturated open `U` (the fibre over `ŷ ∈ Vset`).
351	  have hsecU : ∀ t : X.left, sec.left.base t ∈ (↑U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) := by
352	    intro t
353	    rw [_hUV, Set.mem_preimage]
354	    have e1 : (snd X Y).left.base (sec.left.base t)
355	        = (snd X Y).left.base (q.base (X.hom.base t)) := by
356	      have h2 := congrArg (fun m : X.left ⟶ Y.left => m.base t) hsecLsnd
357	      simpa only [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply] using h2
358	    rw [e1]
359	    have hqmem : q.base (X.hom.base t) ∈ (↑U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) := by
360	      rw [hq, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, pointOfClosedPoint_apply, ← Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
361	      exact Set.mem_range_self x
362	    rw [_hUV, Set.mem_preimage] at hqmem
363	    exact hqmem
364	  have hrange : Set.range ((sec ≫ f).left).base ⊆ Set.range U₀.ι.base := by
365	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
366	    rintro _ ⟨t, rfl⟩
367	    have hfin := _hfU (sec.left.base t) (hsecU t)
368	    rw [Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
369	    exact hfin
370	  set g : X.left ⟶ U₀.toScheme := IsOpenImmersion.lift U₀.ι (sec ≫ f).left hrange with hgdef
371	  have hgfac : g ≫ U₀.ι = (sec ≫ f).left := IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hrange
372	  -- Deep algebra: the two `k̄`-points `xq`, `x₀` of the proper integral slice agree under `sec≫f`.
373	  have key : xq.left ≫ g = x₀.left ≫ g :=
374	    eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral X.hom g xq.left x₀.left (Over.w xq) (Over.w x₀)
375	  -- Over-level: `q̂ ≫ f = xq ≫ sec ≫ f` and `q̂ ≫ retract ≫ f = x₀ ≫ sec ≫ f`.
376	  have hqf : qhat ≫ f = xq ≫ sec ≫ f := by rw [← Category.assoc, ← hIover]
377	  have hqrf : qhat ≫ retract ≫ f = x₀ ≫ sec ≫ f := by
378	    rw [← Category.assoc, hIIover, Category.assoc]
379	  -- Translate to the `q`-form of the goal.
380	  have hxqf : q ≫ f.left = xq.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := by
381	    simpa only [Over.comp_left, hqhatL] using congrArg Over.Hom.left hqf
382	  have hx₀f : q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left = x₀.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := by
383	    simpa only [Over.comp_left, hqhatL] using congrArg Over.Hom.left hqrf
384	  -- The two `k̄`-points have the same image under `sec ≫ f` (corestricted via `key`).
385	  have hbridge : xq.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left = x₀.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := by
386	    rw [← hgfac, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
387	    exact congrArg (· ≫ U₀.ι) key
388	  have hgoalq : q ≫ f.left = q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left :=
389	    hxqf.trans (hbridge.trans hx₀f.symm)
390	  rw [hq] at hgoalq
391	  simpa only [Category.assoc] using hgoalq
392	
393	/-- **Bridge 2 of the Rigidity Lemma (slice-constancy / the agreement equation), the residual
394	geometric input.** Let `X` be complete (proper) over an algebraically closed `k̄`, `x₀` a
395	`k̄`-point of `X`, and `f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` into a separated `Z`. Let `U = p₂⁻¹(V)` be a `p₂`-saturated
396	open of `X ⊗ Y` (the preimage of a set `Vset ⊆ Y`) on which `f` lands inside a single **affine**
397	open `U₀ ⊆ Z`. Then `f` agrees on `U` with the collapsed map `retract ≫ f`
398	(`retract := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`, i.e. `(x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)`):
399	
400	  `U.ι ≫ f.left = U.ι ≫ (retract ≫ f).left`.
401	
402	This is the cohomology-free **route B** of the iter-159 `mathlib-analogist` consult
403	(`analogies/rigidity-affineconst.md`); it was the genuinely-deep residual of the Rigidity-Lemma
404	chain and is now **PROVEN axiom-clean** (iter-162), assembled here as a named top-level obligation
405	from Step 2 (`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`) over the per-slice Step 1
406	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`). The relative Stein-factorisation /
407	proper-pushforward `f_*𝒪 = 𝒪` framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap and is **deliberately avoided**.
408	
409	The intended proof (no coherent cohomology):
410	1. *Per closed slice.* For each closed point `y ∈ Vset`, `κ(y) = k̄` (`[IsAlgClosed kbar]`, finite
411	   type). Saturation puts the whole fibre `X_y` inside `U`, so `f` maps the proper integral slice
412	   `X_y ≅ X` into the affine `U₀`. By `isField_of_universallyClosed` +
413	   `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` + alg-closedness, `Γ(X_y) = k̄`, so the slice maps to a
414	   single `k̄`-point of `U₀`
415	   (`ext_of_isAffine`); that point is `f(x₀, y)`, since `(x₀, y) ∈ X_y`. Hence `f` and `retract ≫ f`
416	   agree at every closed point of `U`.
417	2. *Globalise.* Closed points are dense in the locally-of-finite-type `k̄`-scheme `U`
418	   (`closure_closedPoints`, the Jacobson-space property). Turning "agrees at each closed point"
419	   into one dominant probe (the coproduct `∐_{x∈closedPoints U} Spec κ(x) ⟶ U`, dense range) and
420	   feeding it to `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` (the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity
421	   `rigidity_core` already uses) yields the morphism equality on all of `U`. This last
422	   "dense-closed-points ⟹ hom-ext" connective is the one piece Mathlib does not package directly. -/
423	theorem rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine
424	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
425	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
426	    [IsProper X.hom]
427	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
428	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
429	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
430	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
431	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
432	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
433	    (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens)
434	    (Vset : Set Y.left)
435	    (_hUV : (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) = (snd X Y).left.base ⁻¹' Vset)
436	    (U₀ : Z.left.Opens) (_hU₀ : IsAffineOpen U₀)
437	    (_hfU : ∀ u ∈ (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left), f.left.base u ∈ U₀) :
438	    (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫ f.left =
439	      (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫
440	        (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left := by
441	  -- Target separatedness (absolute), from `IsSeparated Z.hom` and the affine base `Spec k̄`:
442	  -- `terminal.from Z.left = Z.hom ≫ terminal.from (Spec k̄)`, a composite of separated maps.
443	  haveI : Z.left.IsSeparated := by
444	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
445	    have heq : terminal.from Z.left = Z.hom ≫ terminal.from (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) :=
446	      terminal.hom_ext _ _
447	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
448	  -- JACOBSON DERIVATION (iter-161: now a routine instance discharge, NOT an as-typed gap).
449	  -- The route-B globalisation of the per-closed-slice constancy needs the closed points of `U` to
450	  -- be DENSE, i.e. `U` to be a Jacobson space. The chain now carries
451	  -- `[LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]` as a hypothesis of this lemma, so `JacobsonSpace U` is
452	  -- derivable: `Spec k̄` is a Jacobson space (a field is an `IsJacobsonRing`,
453	  -- `PrimeSpectrum.instJacobsonSpaceOfIsJacobsonRing`); `LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace` then
454	  -- transports it to `(X ⊗ Y).left`; and `JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding` inherits it onto the
455	  -- open subscheme `U`. This `sorry` is therefore the assembly of those three Mathlib facts, left
456	  -- for the prover phase — it is no longer an as-typed-unprovability.
457	  haveI : JacobsonSpace ((U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme) := by
458	    -- `Spec k̄` is Jacobson (a field is `IsArtinianRing`, hence `IsJacobsonRing`); transport
459	    -- across the locally-of-finite-type structure map to `(X ⊗ Y).left`; then inherit onto the
460	    -- open subscheme `U` along the open embedding `U.ι`.
461	    haveI : JacobsonSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
462	      LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace (X ⊗ Y).hom
463	    exact JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding U.ι.isOpenEmbedding
464	  -- Globalise the per-closed-point slice-constancy (Step 1,
465	  -- `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`) over the dense closed points (Step 2,
466	  -- `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`). This wires bridge 2's route B end to end.
467	  exact morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints fun x hx =>
468	    rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine f x₀ U Vset _hUV U₀ _hU₀ _hfU x hx
469	
470	/-- **The dense-open agreement (the genuine geometric content).** Mumford's open
471	`X × V` together with the slice-constancy `f(x, y) = f(x₀, y)` on it, packaged as the single
472	existential that `rigidity_core`'s gluing step consumes: there is a non-empty open `U` of
473	`(X ⊗ Y).left` on which `f` and the collapsed map `retract ≫ f` agree as scheme morphisms.
474	
475	**Status (iter-159): this lemma is now `sorry`-free in its own body.** The construction of the
476	non-empty open `U = X × V` (closed-map bridge 1, `snd_left_isClosedMap`) and its non-emptiness
477	(`y₀ ∉ G` via the collapse hypothesis `_hf`, using the pullback-fibre fact `hfib` over the
478	`k̄`-point `y₀`) are discharged here. The one remaining geometric input — slice-constancy on `U`
479	(bridge 2) — is delegated to the named helper `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, now
480	assembled from the proven Step 2 (`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`) over the per-slice Step 1
481	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`); its affine-containment hypothesis is read off
482	the definition of `G` inside this proof. The whole chain — including Step 1's geometric
483	slice/section assembly — is now PROVEN axiom-clean (iter-162).
484	
485	This lemma carries the **collapse hypothesis** `_hf : f(X × {y₀}) = {z₀}` (encoded
486	`lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀`), which is exactly what makes Mumford's open
487	`V := Y ∖ G` non-empty (`y₀ ∉ G`, since the rigidified slice `f(X × {y₀}) = {z₀} ⊆ U` lands in
488	the affine `U`, so its image under `snd` avoids `G = snd '' (f ⁻¹ F)`). Without `_hf` the lemma
489	is **false** (e.g. `f := fst : X ⊗ Y ⟶ X = Z` has no open of agreement). The full instance set
490	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `IsReduced`, `IsSeparated`) and `_hf` only strengthen the
491	antecedent.
492	
493	Of the two char-free Mathlib bridges of `rigidity_core`'s docstring, **bridge 1** (the closed-map
494	argument, `IsProper.toUniversallyClosed` ⟹ the projection is closed) is discharged here — it
495	produces the non-empty open `U = X × V`. **Bridge 2** (the affine-constancy argument,
496	`isField_of_universallyClosed` on each proper integral slice mapping to an affine, supplying the
497	scheme-level equality on `U`) is the residual content, isolated in the named helper
498	`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`. -/
499	theorem rigidity_eqOn_dense_open
500	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
501	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
502	    [IsProper X.hom]
503	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
504	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
505	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
506	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
507	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
508	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
509	    (y₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Y)
510	    (z₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Z)
511	    (_hf : lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀) :
512	    ∃ U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens, (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left).Nonempty ∧
513	      (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫ f.left =
514	        (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫
515	          (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left := by
516	  -- Mumford's construction of the open `U = X × V`, `V = Y - G`, `G = p₂(f⁻¹(Z - U₀))`.
517	  -- Bridge 1 (`snd_left_isClosedMap`) makes `G` closed; the collapse hypothesis `_hf` makes
518	  -- `y₀ ∉ G` so `V` (hence `U`) is non-empty; bridge 2 (affine-constancy) is the agreement.
519	  have hclosed : IsClosedMap (snd X Y).left.base := snd_left_isClosedMap
520	  -- `Spec k̄` is a single point; transport the instance to the monoidal unit's underlying scheme.
521	  haveI hsub : Subsingleton (↥(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)))).left) :=
522	    inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)))
523	  -- The chosen `k̄`-point of `Spec k̄` (`= (𝟙_).left`).
524	  have ptk : (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)))).left :=
525	    (inferInstance : Inhabited (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))).default
526	  -- The image point `z₀` in `Z` and an affine open neighbourhood `U₀ ∋ z₀`.
527	  let z₀pt : Z.left := z₀.left.base ptk
528	  obtain ⟨U₀, _hU₀aff, hz₀U₀, -⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := Z.left)
529	    (x := z₀pt) (U := ⊤) trivial
530	  -- `G = p₂(f⁻¹(Z - U₀))` is closed (image of a closed set under the closed map `p₂`).
531	  set Gset := (snd X Y).left.base '' (f.left.base ⁻¹' (U₀ : Set Z.left)ᶜ) with hGdef
532	  have hG : IsClosed Gset := hclosed _ (U₀.isOpen.isClosed_compl.preimage f.left.base.hom.2)
533	  -- `U = p₂⁻¹(Y - G)` (Mumford's `X × V`) is open.
534	  have hUopen : IsOpen ((snd X Y).left.base ⁻¹' Gsetᶜ) :=
535	    (hG.isOpen_compl).preimage (snd X Y).left.base.hom.2
536	  -- The slice section `s : X → X ⊗ Y`, `x ↦ (x, y₀)`, and the points `y₀`, `x₀`.
537	  let s := (lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀)).left
538	  let y₀pt : Y.left := y₀.left.base ptk
539	  let x₀pt : X.left := x₀.left.base ptk
540	  -- Every point of the slice `p₂⁻¹{y₀}` lies in the image of the section `s`. True because `y₀`
541	  -- is a `k̄`-point: the slice section `s : X → X ⊗ Y` exhibits `X.left` as the fibre of the
542	  -- pullback projection `p₂` over `y₀`. We paste the identity (iso) outer square for `s` against
543	  -- the canonical pullback square and read the fibre off the coarse `PullbackCarrier` layer
544	  -- (`Scheme.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback`); no residue fields / `Triplet` machinery.
545	  have hfib : (snd X Y).left.base ⁻¹' {y₀pt} ⊆ Set.range s.base := by
546	    set p₁ := pullback.fst X.hom Y.hom with hp₁def
547	    set p₂ := pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom with hp₂def
548	    -- `(toUnit X).left = X.hom`: the structure map of the unit is `𝟙`, and `Over.w` collapses.
549	    have htoUnit : (toUnit X).left = X.hom := by simp
550	    -- Triangle identities of the slice section `s = (x ↦ (x, y₀)).left`.
551	    have hsp1 : s ≫ p₁ = 𝟙 X.left := by
552	      rw [hp₁def, ← Over.fst_left, ← Over.comp_left, lift_fst, Over.id_left]
553	    have hsp2 : s ≫ p₂ = X.hom ≫ y₀.left := by
554	      rw [hp₂def, ← Over.snd_left, ← Over.comp_left, lift_snd, Over.comp_left]
555	      exact congrArg (· ≫ y₀.left) htoUnit
556	    -- `y₀` is a section of `Y.hom` (it is a `k̄`-point of `Y`).
557	    have hsec : y₀.left ≫ Y.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of kbar)) := by simpa using Over.w y₀
558	    -- The outer square `(s ≫ p₁ ; X.hom) = (X.hom ; y₀.left ≫ Y.hom)` is a pullback: both
559	    -- horizontal legs are identities (isos).
560	    have houter : IsPullback (s ≫ p₁) X.hom X.hom (y₀.left ≫ Y.hom) := by
561	      have hiso : IsPullback (𝟙 X.left) X.hom X.hom (𝟙 (Spec (.of kbar))) :=
562	        IsPullback.of_horiz_isIso ⟨by simp⟩
563	      rwa [← hsp1, ← hsec] at hiso
564	    -- Paste off the canonical right pullback square to recover the left square `hL`.
565	    have hL : IsPullback s X.hom p₂ y₀.left :=
566	      IsPullback.of_right houter hsp2 (IsPullback.of_hasPullback X.hom Y.hom)
567	    -- Range of `s` = fibre of `p₂` over `range y₀.left`, via the coarse pullback-carrier lemma.
568	    have hrange : Set.range s.base = p₂.base ⁻¹' Set.range y₀.left.base := by
569	      simpa [Set.image_univ, Set.preimage_univ] using
570	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback hL.flip Set.univ
571	    rw [Over.snd_left, ← hp₂def, hrange]
572	    exact Set.preimage_mono (Set.singleton_subset_iff.mpr ⟨ptk, rfl⟩)
573	  -- `y₀ ∉ G`: any point over `y₀` is `s x`, and `_hf` collapses `f (s x) = z₀ ∈ U₀`.
574	  have hy₀ : y₀pt ∉ Gset := by
575	    rintro ⟨q, hq, hsndq⟩
576	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hfib (by simpa using hsndq)
577	    apply hq
578	    have hcomp : s ≫ f.left = (toUnit X ≫ z₀).left := by
579	      rw [← Over.comp_left]; exact congrArg Over.Hom.left _hf
580	    have hfx : f.left.base (s.base x) = z₀pt := by
581	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, hcomp, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
582	      change z₀.left.base ((toUnit X).left.base x) = z₀.left.base ptk
583	      congr 1; exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
584	    rw [hfx]; exact hz₀U₀
585	  -- Assemble `U`, witness its non-emptiness by `s x₀` (which lies over `y₀ ∈ V`).
586	  refine ⟨⟨_, hUopen⟩, ⟨s.base x₀pt, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
587	  · change (snd X Y).left.base (s.base x₀pt) ∈ Gsetᶜ
588	    have hsnd : (snd X Y).left.base (s.base x₀pt) = y₀pt := by
589	      have hcomp : s ≫ (snd X Y).left = (toUnit X ≫ y₀).left := by
590	        rw [← Over.comp_left]; exact congrArg Over.Hom.left (lift_snd (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀))
591	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, hcomp, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
592	      change y₀.left.base ((toUnit X).left.base x₀pt) = y₀.left.base ptk
593	      congr 1; exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
594	    rw [Set.mem_compl_iff, hsnd]; exact hy₀
595	  · -- Bridge 2 (affine-constancy): on `U = X × V` (saturated: `U = p₂⁻¹ Gsetᶜ`) `f` lands in the
596	    -- affine `U₀`, so each proper slice maps to a single point `f(x₀, y)` and `f` agrees with
597	    -- `retract ≫ f` on `U`. The affine-containment `hfU` is read off the definition of `Gset`;
598	    -- the slice-constancy + dense-closed-points globalisation is the residual `route B`, isolated
599	    -- as the named helper `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine` (cohomology-free, ≈1–2 iter).
600	    have hfU : ∀ u ∈ ((⟨_, hUopen⟩ : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens) : Set (X ⊗ Y).left),
601	        f.left.base u ∈ U₀ := by
602	      intro u hu
603	      by_contra hcon
604	      -- `u ∈ U` means `p₂ u ∉ Gset`; but `f u ∉ U₀` puts `p₂ u` into `Gset = p₂ '' (f⁻¹ U₀ᶜ)`.
605	      exact hu ⟨u, hcon, rfl⟩
606	    exact rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine f x₀ ⟨_, hUopen⟩ Gsetᶜ rfl U₀ _hU₀aff hfU

608	/-- **Geometric core of the Rigidity Lemma (PROVEN axiom-clean, iter-162).** With
609	`X` complete (proper) and `x₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ X` a `k̄`-point, the morphism `f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` is invariant
610	under the "collapse-the-`X`-axis-onto-`x₀`" endomorphism
611	`retract := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`:
612	
613	  `f = retract ≫ f`,
614	
615	i.e. `f(x, y) = f(x₀, y)` for all `(x, y)` — `f` depends only on the `Y`-coordinate. This is
616	the entirety of the *geometric* content of Mumford's Rigidity Lemma (Form I); the rest of
617	`rigidity_lemma` is the cartesian-monoidal algebra discharged by `rigidity_snd_lift`.
618	
619	This core is now **proven** by the project's scheme-level rigidity (replicated inline from
620	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`, since the wrapper `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`
621	lives in the downstream `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`): two maps out of the geometrically
622	irreducible reduced `X ⊗ Y` into the separated `Z` that agree on a non-empty open agree
623	everywhere. The existence of that non-empty open together with the agreement on it —
624	`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`, the actual geometry below — is now itself PROVEN axiom-clean.
625	
626	This core carries the **collapse hypothesis** `_hf : f(X × {y₀}) = {z₀}` (encoded
627	`lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀`), passed straight through to
628	`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`: it is exactly what makes Mumford's open `V := Y ∖ G` non-empty
629	(`y₀ ∉ G`). Without it the core statement is **false** (`f := fst` is not collapse-invariant).
630	
631	## Mumford's proof of `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` (Abelian Varieties, Ch. II §4, p. 43)
632	
633	Set `g(y) = f(x₀, y)` (so `retract ≫ f` is exactly `(x, y) ↦ g(y)`). The open `V` is produced as
634	follows: let `U` be an affine open neighbourhood of a chosen point in `Z`, `F = Z ∖ U`, and
635	`G = (snd X Y) '' (f ⁻¹ F)`. Then for each `y ∈ V := Y ∖ G`, the slice `f(X × {y}) ⊆ U`, and `f`

636	agrees with `retract ≫ f` on the non-empty open `X × V`.
637	
638	This rests on **two char-free Mathlib bridges** (the cube-free heart); bridge 1 is **built**,
639	bridge 2 (`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`) is **decomposed and assembled** — Step 2
640	proven (`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`), Step 1's deep algebra proven
641	(`eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral`), Step 1's geometric slice/section assembly
642	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`) also proven via `isIntegral_of_retract`:
643	
644	  1. **Properness ⇒ the projection is a closed map — BUILT** (`snd_left_isClosedMap`, iter-158).
645	     `IsProper X.hom` is universally closed (`AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.toUniversallyClosed`), and
646	     `snd X Y` is the base change of `X.hom` along `Y.hom`, hence universally closed, hence a closed
647	     map. The glue identifying the monoidal `snd X Y` in `Over (Spec k̄)` with the scheme-theoretic
648	     pullback projection `Limits.pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom` is the exact rewrite `Over.snd_left`, and
649	     `IsClosedMap` is transported across the canonical pullback square via
650	     `universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange`. So `G = snd '' (closed)` is closed, `V` open.
651	
652	  2. **A proper connected variety mapping to an affine has image a single point.** For `y ∈ V`,
653	     the proper connected slice `X × {y}` maps under `f` into the affine `U`; a global regular
654	     function on a proper integral `k̄`-scheme is constant. Mathlib *has* the key fact:
655	     `AlgebraicGeometry.isField_of_universallyClosed` — for `X` integral and `f : X ⟶ Spec K`
656	     universally closed (`K` a field), `Γ(X, ⊤)` is a field. Combined with `Γ(affine U) → Γ(X×{y})`
657	     being a `k̄`-algebra map into a field that is finite over `k̄`
658	     (`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` under `LocallyOfFiniteType`) and
659	     `k̄` algebraically closed, the map `X × {y} → U` factors through a single point. This is the
660	     "global-sections-constant" argument; assembling it into "image is one point" is the
661	     remaining work.
662	
663	These are exactly the "two Mathlib bridges to find/build" flagged in `PROGRESS.md`. Both bridges
664	are char-free (no theorem of the cube, no cohomology beyond `H⁰`). Bridge 1 is built
665	(`snd_left_isClosedMap`) and the non-emptiness fibre fact (`hfib`) is closed, so
666	`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` is `sorry`-free; bridge 2 (slice-constancy) is decomposed and
667	assembled (Steps 1–2), with Step 1's geometric slice/section assembly
668	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`) now PROVEN. The categorical reduction
669	(`rigidity_lemma`, `rigidity_snd_lift`) and the scheme-level gluing (`rigidity_core`) are closed —
670	the entire chain is axiom-clean (iter-162). -/
671	theorem rigidity_core
672	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
673	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
674	    [IsProper X.hom]
675	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
676	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
677	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
678	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
679	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
680	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
681	    (y₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Y)
682	    (z₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Z)
683	    (_hf : lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀) :
684	    f = lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f := by
685	  -- The gluing step is scheme-level rigidity: two maps out of a geometrically irreducible reduced
686	  -- source into a separated target that agree on a non-empty open agree everywhere. We replicate
687	  -- the argument of `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`'s `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` inline (that
688	  -- file is *downstream* of this one, so its wrapper is unavailable here) directly from Mathlib's
689	  -- `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The non-empty open and the agreement on it are the
690	  -- genuine geometric content, isolated in `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`.
691	  obtain ⟨U, hU, h⟩ := rigidity_eqOn_dense_open f x₀ y₀ z₀ _hf
692	  -- `Spec k̄` is a single point, so geometric irreducibility makes `(X ⊗ Y).left` irreducible.
693	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
694	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ⊗ Y).hom
695	  -- A non-empty open of an irreducible space is dense, so its inclusion is dominant.
696	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) :=
697	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
698	  -- Provide separatedness of `Z.left` over `Spec k̄` in the `OverClass.fromOver` form.
699	  haveI : IsSeparated (Z.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := ‹IsSeparated Z.hom›
700	  -- Promote the underlying-scheme equality to an `Over (Spec k̄)` equality.
701	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
702	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
703	    (X := (X ⊗ Y).left) (Y := Z.left) (f := f.left)
704	    (g := (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left) U.ι h
705	
706	/-- **Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I).** Let `X` be a complete (proper) variety and `Y`, `Z`
707	any varieties over `k̄`. If `f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` collapses the slice `X × {y₀}` to a single point
708	`z₀` (encoded as `lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀`), then `f` factors through
709	the second projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y`, i.e. there is `g : Y ⟶ Z` with `f = snd ≫ g`.
710	
711	This is the cube-free, cohomology-free entry point of the chain: its only inputs are that
712	completeness of `X` makes the projection a closed map, and that a proper connected variety has
713	no nonconstant map to an affine variety. Valid in arbitrary characteristic.
